programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble dates a waitress whose nephew's buddies plan to capture him with a mail-order rifle

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A routine investigation at a crime scene turns sour when Danny is attacked by a criminal hiding in the victim's house. He gives chase to the subway, where shots are fired - during which the offender vanishes and a police officer is killed, leaving Danny as prime suspect. Meanwhile, Stella investigates the discovery of a nanny's body in a Central Park public lavatory. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects foul play when a seaman who was set to testify before a grand jury dies before he can make his court appearance. When it becomes clear the man was murdered, the subsequent investigation reveals that a previously thwarted human-trafficking ring has been re-established. Meanwhile, a storm causes a massive power outage, and McGee seeks a room for the night among his colleagues. Naval crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A vehicle transporting Jada Khaled to her criminal tribunal is ambushed, prompting the team to trace the culprit in an attempt to save her and the case. programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble discovers that negligence caused the death of a salvage diver and jeopardises his own life when he befriends the girl accused of being a jinx

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A jeweller returning to Los Angeles from South America is charged with murder when the body of a notorious international jewel thief is found in his car

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman battles a formidable arms dealer in a fight to the death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a diplomatic service van is hijacked during the transportation of an unknown package from the State Department. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, the entire team is put in danger while hunting for the killer of a former NCIS agent. programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must work with an old nemesis, Mark Collins. Meanwhile, Walter and Paige awkwardly navigate their new relationship

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy recruitment officer is murdered at a high school and the agents discover he had recently begun a relationship with a married teacher whose wife held a grudge against him. They also find out a student's father was vehemently opposed to his joining the military, and receive help from an unexpected quarter - Ducky's esteemed predecessor, Dr Magnus. Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former serviceman who had been having an affair is beaten to death in his own back garden, and the agents are forced to consider the possibility that his wife - a US Marine and martial arts expert - is the killer. Meanwhile, McGee becomes the subject of an embarrassing mix-up when an inflatable doll apparently purchased on his credit card is delivered. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are forced to undergo psychological evaluations conducted by Dr Rachel Cranston (guest star Wendy Makkena) while investigating the death of a submarine's commanding officer. Suspicion falls on a journalist seen arguing with the man before his demise, and Tony discovers a link between the psychotherapist shadowing the agents and Ziva's predecessor Kate Todd. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

Elsewhere, as the holiday season approaches, Bishop and Jake discuss the future of their relationship. Naval crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.desc: The first instalment of a two-part crossover with NCIS: New Orleans sees the Washington, DC team co-operating with their colleagues from the Big Easy to investigate a spate of lethal poisonings on board a New Orleans-to-DC flight. Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula

programme.desc: Tom and Diana grow bored with their cosy lifestyles, and decide to look for jobs. Meanwhile, Bayview boss Harvey comes under attack for not providing residents with adequate heating. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden as the retirement home delinquents

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Luke arrives back home in a wheelchair, but quickly becomes frustrated and takes his anger out on David. Surrounded by the French, he manages to pull off an inspiring propaganda coup in the small town of Torrecastro. Swashbuckling adventure, featuring Downton Abbey creator Julian Fellowes and starring Sean Bean and Brian Cox

programme.desc: The son of an overthrown king must embark on an epic sea voyage in search of the fabled Golden Fleece so he can return and reclaim his kingdom. Before he can seize the treasure, he has to overcome a series of horrific creatures, including a seven-headed monster and a horde of sword-wielding skeletons. Mythological fantasy adventure, starring Todd Armstrong, Honor Blackman, Nancy Kovack, Niall MacGinnis and Patrick Troughton, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's knight in shining armour arrives in the form of paroled rock legend Abel Kane, whisking them off on a winter break to Majorca, where yet more unexpected good fortune awaits them. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie discovers a medieval recipe for traditional liquor among some ancient scrolls and goes into full-scale production, building a distillery in his barn and buying up all the potatoes he can find. The business goes well, but his success attracts some unwelcome visitors - the Driscoll brothers. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie senses an opportunity to get himself knighted when Marlene becomes heavily involved in charity work. Enlisting the help of their staff, Marlene tries to make it happen using her contacts, but achieving her husband's dream turns out to be harder than expected. Comedy, starring John Challis

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Animal rights activists target the village, with Llewellyn falling victim to a paint bomb and the pub coming under threat, so Boycie organises patrol groups to protect Winderdown farm from attack. Meanwhile, Tyler wants to borrow money to buy equipment for his band, but Boycie refuses until his son completes a task to prove his business acumen

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When a pampered dog runs away from a life of luxury, Frank and Lu are asked to track it down. Comedy drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: DI John Rebus investigates a murder that looks set to result in gang warfare, but his cynical inquiries come to an abrupt halt when his daughter Sammy is involved in a near-fatal hit-and-run incident - leading a vengeful Rebus on an obsessive trail that threatens to engulf him. Crime thriller, starring John Hannah and Eilidh MacDonald

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The strange activities of a religious cult prompt Jardine and Reid to investigate a series of murders committed by a killer obsessed with the biblical plagues of Egypt. Glasgow-set detective drama, starring Blythe Duff and James MacPherson

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Drama about military police and their families faced with political and personal conflicts in a 1960s British colony on the Red Sea coast. An officer and his wife arrive in Aden, where he is to replace a much-loved captain. He soon has to adjust to life on the frontline, along with resentment among the men that he was appointed instead of the already-present Lt Ed Laithwaite. Honor is taken under the wing of Laithwaite's wife, Alison, who had been having an affair with Captain Page. Starring Jessie Buckley and Jeremy Neumark Jones

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Mary goes into labour while her husband has left the city to rendezvous with the SAS, who are returning to Aden after a secret mission to capture a dangerous fugitive. When things go badly wrong, Harry must choose between his men and his family. An American reporter ingratiates herself with the troops, to Joe's annoyance, and Honor joins an exclusive club

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sally and Beth discover a man found badly beaten had argued with his runaway daughter Lucy the night before. Beth develops a soft spot for the girl, but can't get much out of her about the incident and her touchingly protective boyfriend Mark (guest star Joe Absolom) denies suggestions he might be involved. The family clearly have a lot to hide and Sally warns Beth not to get personally involved

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Lucy Burton's body is found in an apparent suicide, but forensics reveal she was murdered. A distraught Beth has the unenviable task of quizzing her friends and searching the house, while Terry discovers the victim's sinister housemate Mark Lawrence (played by Joe Absolom) has a history of beguiling and controlling girls

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New constables Leon Taylor, Millie Brown and Arun Ghir (Dominic Power, Clare Foster and Abhin Galeya) quickly get into their stride when a stolen cutting torch is used in a bank robbery. Leon makes his first arrest as the bumbling duo who tunnelled into the bank are traced, but the money has disappeared and the mastermind behind the operation is still on the loose

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A woman's body is found on a landfill site and Neil and Kezia learn the victim had been involved in an argument at a house party on the night of her death. Three suspects emerge from the story of her tangled love life, but the investigation reaches deadlock when all three seem to have cast-iron alibis

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: It's Christmas time in Poplar and love is in the air as Jenny's relationship with Alec continues to blossom, while Shelagh is planning her quiet wedding to Dr Turner. However, the peace is suddenly disturbed when the police arrive at Nonnatus House to announce an unexploded bomb has been discovered just around the corner and everybody in the district is being evacuated to safety. The electricity has been cut off and the streets are pitch-black, and with the locals scurrying to safety, the nuns and nurses reach out to anyone in need of medical help. The spirit of the Blitz takes over as the community's members pull together - aware that, at any moment, their homes could be reduced to rubble. Jessica Raine, Jenny Agutter, Pam Ferris and Laura Main star in a festive special of the 1950s-set drama, with a guest appearance by comedian Sandi Toksvig

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives move into their new premises following the explosion that wrecked the old Nonnatus House, and are joined by fresh arrival Sister Winifred - a kindly-but-naive young woman. Jenny becomes concerned about new mother Merle's mental state and the welfare of her baby, who isn't feeding properly. Dr Turner believes it could be something more serious - although the mystery is eventually solved by the last person anyone would have expected. Chummy is struggling to adjust to her new role as a housewife and mother, and organises an open day at the community centre. Jessica Raine, Stephen McGann and Miranda Hart star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie's feathers are ruffled when Jenny is promoted, but the new Acting Sister has more to worry about after a mother-to-be reveals the baby she is carrying was not fathered by her husband - and to complicate matters, the real father is black. Cynthia is inspired by a lecture on natural childbirth, convinced it will help an overly anxious patient, while Alec and his friends try to persuade Jenny to join them on a CND march. Jessica Raine, Helen George and Bryony Hannah star in the 1950s-set nursing drama

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's A Dinner of Herbs
programme.desc: First in a two-part drama, starring Billie Whitelaw as a herbalist who takes an 11-year-old orphan under her wing. The youngster soon makes friends with a local girl and the future looks rosy - but heartbreak is waiting just around the corner. With Tim Healy, Jonathan Kerrigan and Michael Jayston

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A distant cousin of Fred's who has Down's syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his protective mother, but Violet struggles to cope and Shelagh is forced to make other arrangements for his care. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House, and Trixie comes to the aid of a pregnant woman who is in urgent need of dental treatment to avoid an infection. The nurses discover what has happened to Sister Mary Cynthia, a revelation that has distressing consequences for Sister Monica Joan

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A sailor's trinket box and a doll also receive some much-needed attention

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.desc: Now the main man on BBC magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer. However, conscious of being left in the shadow of co-presenter Jennie and alert to potential changes behind the scenes, he grows ever more anxious about his place in the big time. Return of the media-based comedy, starring Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring appearances by Tom Cruise, Rebel Wilson, Rege-Jean Page, Rosamund Pike, Tom Jones, Keeley Hawes, Ian Wright, Sienna Miller, Sam Neill, Kate Winslet, Alan Carr, Felicity Kendal, Adrian Lester, Amy Poehler, Daniel Kaluuya, Jennifer Garner, Stanley Tucci, Cush Jumbo, Liam Neeson, Octavia Spencer, Melissa McCarthy and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Blair Witch Project (1999)
programme.desc: Three film students head into the American backwoods to make a documentary about the fabled Blair Witch. There proves to be more to the urban legend than meets the eye, and as night falls they find themselves in very real danger. Horror, presented as camcorder footage shot by the characters during their investigation, starring Heather Donahue, Michael Williams and Joshua Leonard

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Former Great British Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain is in East Anglia as she continues her culinary road trip around Britain, going out to sea with Norfolk fisherman Richard Matthews to catch crabs for Vietnamese-style summer rolls. She then heads to Ely in Cambridgeshire to meet potato farmer and crisp factory owner Ross Taylor, using his snacks to make a crisp, chocolate and salted peanut dessert. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a ploughman's cheese and pickle tart, and a herby chicken and potato salad

programme.title: Football Focus

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Coverage of the fourth and concluding session of the opening semi-final, where the first player to reach 17 frames will progress. Kyren Wilson booked his place in the final last year in the most dramatic of circumstances, with the deciding frame lasting 62 minutes and breaking the record for the most combined points scored in a single frame at the Crucible, with Wilson eventually winning it 103-83

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Debbie McGee, Ben Cajee, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are Dame Margot Fonteyn, West Ham United, Inside No.9, and the women of Imperial Rome. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as writers and illustrators Steve Cavanagh, Paul Tonkinson, Emma Kennedy, Jen Brister, Kate Mosse, Adele Parks, Chris Riddell, and Lemn Sissay take part in the general knowledge quiz. The contestants try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are a father and daughter from Northamptonshire, and couples from Lancashire and Rotherham. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Fleur East, as they assist two friends from London in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing acrobat, a princess and a karate champion

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan resolves to confess her drug smuggling to the police. Terrified that Jan is about to throw her life away, Iain contacts Ross in prison and urges him to step in to save her. Jacob is determined to save a woman who has fallen through a skylight, but feelings run high concerning the patient when she is revealed to have a white supremacist tattoo. Feeling dejected at missing out on the paramedic position, Leon decides to leave Holby

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose tries to force Faith into helping her with the repossession of Cindy's salon, unaware that her daughter is working with the police to gather evidence against her. Her campaign of revenge takes an even more personal turn when she kidnaps Alys and Megan from school and reveals their mother's darkest secret. Osian falls into a coma, and the doctors do not expect him to recover, but Faith refuses to give up on him and insists on pressing ahead with the court hearing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Fulham at Craven Cottage. The Citizens will have been looking to emulate their 4-0 victory over Palace in January's reverse fixture and move a step closer to the title, while added spice was added to the London derby due to Chelsea needing the points to boost their top four hopes and Fulham battling for survival. Plus, Everton v Aston Villa and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leeds United

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most popular cult heroes in Premier League history

programme.title: Flying Blind (2012)
programme.desc: An aerospace engineer designing military drones begins a passionate affair with a younger Muslim student. The relationship leads to her being questioned by the security services, who claim he is involved in terrorism, forcing her to question how much she knows about her lover and where her loyalties lie. Drama, showing in tribute to the late Helen McCrory. With Najib Oudghiri and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Fulham at Craven Cottage. The Citizens will have been looking to emulate their 4-0 victory over Palace in January's reverse fixture and move a step closer to the title, while added spice was added to the London derby due to Chelsea needing the points to boost their top four hopes and Fulham battling for survival. Plus, Everton v Aston Villa and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leeds United

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley discusses the importance of wellbeing, meeting therapy dogs that help children learn to read and visiting the UK's first Muslim Ballet School

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: Seven celebrities don their backpacks to find out whether a medieval pilgrimage still has relevance today, taking on a 780km trek across northern Spain over 15 days. Actor Neil Morrissey, entertainer Debbie McGee, comedian Ed Byrne, singer Heather Small, the Rev Kate Bottley, journalist Raphael Rowe and TV presenter JJ Chalmers start by walking the most gruelling part of the Camino, a 26km hike, following a mountain pass from France into Spain that takes them to a height of 1,400m. Each day ends with a stay in a basic hostel, sleeping in dormitories on bunkbeds. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss offer their advice as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Lincoln Antiques Fair, prior to auction in Grantham. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross asks whether silver has lost its shine

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum and Sean Fletcher mark the 100th anniversary of Northern Ireland, meeting Christians from all denominations. Featuring a special performance by Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Bill (2015)
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is determined to become a successful playwright after getting kicked out of a folk band. Heading to London to seek his fortune, he gets caught up in a plot by the king of Spain to blow up Elizabeth I. Historical comedy with the cast of Horrible Histories, starring Mathew Baynton and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following five of the world's most endangered species as they struggle to preserve their social groups, beginning with a chimpanzee troop in Senegal. As the dry season sets in, the Alpha of the pack, who has ruled unchallenged for the past three years, faces a series of challenges and finds little support from the other apes

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven celebrates 50 years as a BBC presenter and his 1250th episode of Countryfile by revisiting parts of the country that hold special memories for him. He uncovers hidden treasures in his childhood Yorkshire home, learns about peasant poet John Clare in Cambridgeshire, and visits an ancient Scottish battlefield

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts a special episode reflecting on the aftermath of the Second World War filmed at Coventry Cathedral, in which people bring in items that date back to the end of the conflict and reveal the personal stories behind them. Featuring a ballgown and a set of handmade playing cards that offer a glimpse into the final days of the war in the Far East, a fragile teacup that survived the bombing of Dresden, and a scrapbook documenting life in post-war Berlin

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Sister Hilda are both bewildered and concerned by a patient's unexplained symptoms, and try to get to the bottom of her ill health before it impacts her pregnancy. A woman expecting her third child is worried about her recent weight gain and turns to Lucille for support, leading to an unexpected discovery

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: With time running out, AC-12 attempt to unmask H, the fourth individual commanding the network of corrupt officers behind the murder of Gail Vella. However, sinister and powerful forces appear intent on orchestrating a cover-up. Steve Oram (Sightseers) guest stars in the final episode of the current series

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Liverpool at Old Trafford. Liverpool had not won here since 2014 and United had already recorded a home win against them in the FA Cup this term, but the last two league meetings at this venue had ended in draws. Plus, Newcastle United v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Bristol City v Manchester United and Everton v Arsenal

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Four people claim to be an artist called Erica, living with partner Sam in a stylish property in south-east London. Panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix and guest Katie Piper are tasked with determining which is the true Erica, as each of the quartet airs their opinion on the real Erica's taste in fashion, her passion for Sam, the story of their wedding day and the name of their plumber. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Woking's Recycling Centre, and singles out a retro swivel chair, a large oak dining table, and a once-cherished sideboard for some restoration work

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dom Chinea tackles the challenge of a battered old barber's pole and Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a significant early work by eminent Nigerian artist Ben Enwonwu

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Coverage of the morning session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first semi-final continues, with eight frames scheduled to take place

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Shaun Murphy. Coverage of the afternoon session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the second semi-final continues, with eight frames scheduled to take place. Mark Selby was in good form against Ronnie O'Sullivan in last year's corresponding session, winning six frames to take a 9-7 lead in the match. However, it was 'the Rocket' who would eventually win the match and go on to win this title for the sixth time in his career, taking him to joint second on the all-time list

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. In Basingstoke he visits a glorious neo-Gothic stately home set in the North Wessex Downs

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Further coverage of the evening session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the third session of the first semi-final takes place. Both last-four matches were classics last year, with Kyren Wilson and Ronnie O'Sullivan each prevailing 17-16, against Anthony McGill and Selby respectively

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They serve the panel of judges, and one of Britain's best known designers, Thomas Heatherwick. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don give a masterclass on growing Mediterranean herbs, plants lily bulbs for summer scent, and in the vegetable garden, he begins to sow seeds for salad root crops

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Nigel Havers, Alex Brooker, Sally Lindsay, Georgia Toffolo and Desiree Burch play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories. With Faisal Islam

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from day 14 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the third session of the opening semi-final - Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: With time running out for Starbuck and her crew to find Earth, she decides to run the risk of accepting an offer of truce from Leoben. Meanwhile, Chief Tyrol remains haunted by Callie's death, Gaius broadcasts his preaching via the radio and President Roslin's cancer treatment becomes more intensive

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: In an attempt to discover the way to Earth, Starbuck accompanies Leoben to the Cylon base ship, where the Hybrid issues her with a cryptic warning. Meanwhile, Admiral Adama becomes anxious that the pilot has not yet returned and President Roslin bonds with another cancer patient. Katee Sackhoff, Edward James Olmos and Mary McDonnell star

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: As the pandemic continues, people are increasingly looking for someone to blame, and asking whether life can ever return to normal. Covid-19 deniers become more numerous and vocal, as alternative and conspiratorial thought battles conventional science-based reason

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield look back at some of the dens they have built throughout the series, and reminisce about the children they have helped

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey and Mwaksy take part in an extreme mountain-biking challenge

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: The show's extras become zombies and want to take over the programme, so Amelia and her friends have 18 minutes to save the world

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: The cast reflect on the defining moments of the show in a distant, post-apocalyptic future

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Ecuador in search of ocean giants and the biggest baby animal on Earth

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Me and My Dog: The Ultimate Contest
programme.desc: Dog owners and their pets compete in the final stage of the contest, taking place in the Lake District and hosted by Chris Packham. First, the Red and Yellow teams face a difficult canine intelligence test which involves Lake Windermere and some dog toys, with one pair proving just how far they have come. Then, the remaining competitors must take on a joint triathlon challenge, involving a two-kilometre bike ride, a 100 metre open water swim, and a 1.5 kilometre cross country run

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Shaun Murphy. Coverage from day 15 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the third and penultimate session of the second semi-final

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Paul Cunningham, Phelim O'Hagan, Gemma Austin and Andy Scollick compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Given Belfast's renowned connection with the Titanic, there is much luxury maritime inspiration to be drawn, but the chefs also plunder Northern Ireland's other long and varied list of inventions

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs compete over dishes inspired by science and innovation, drawing especially from Northern Ireland's legacy of inventions. But whose inventions in the kitchen will tough veteran judge, Daniel Clifford score highest? Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by American actor and director Orson Welles, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: Citizen Kane (1941)
programme.desc: After the death of an influential newspaper magnate, an inquisitive reporter begins piecing together the details of his life, discovering an epic tale of ambition and conflict - all the while trying to discover the meaning behind the millionaire's enigmatic last word. Oscar-winning drama, written, directed by and starring Orson Welles. With Joseph Cotten, Dorothy Comingore and William Alland

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Further coverage of the fourth and concluding session of the opening semi-final, where the first player to reach 17 frames will progress. Kyren Wilson booked his place in the final last year in the most dramatic of circumstances, with the deciding frame lasting 62 minutes and breaking the record for the most combined points scored in a single frame at the Crucible, with Wilson eventually winning it 103-83

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from a Gloucester as Catherine Southon and Michael Baggott select the best antiques and collectables to take to auction

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: The six remaining contestants return to the workshop to face two more challenges, making a cocktail ring before designing a bold bib necklace for drag queen Amrou to wear to a solo show. Who will impress the judges enough to stay, and who will have to leave?

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Shaun Murphy. Coverage from day 15 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the fourth and final session of the second semi-final. Ronnie O'Sullivan went into last year's corresponding session trailing 13-11 to Mark Selby, but used all his talent and experience to eventually prevail 17-16 and reach the final for the seventh time in his career. The Rocket's opponent in the final was Kyren Wilson, who also edged through in a thrilling semi-final against Anthony McGill, to reach the showpiece encounter for the first time in his career. With so many seeds safely navigating the notoriously tricky opening round this year, there's every chance of more drama and quality from the top drawer this time around

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career

programme.title: Bowie at the BBC
programme.desc: A chronology of clips from the BBC archive giving an overview of Bowie's career from 1964 until his death in 2016, offering a series of snapshots into his rapidly evolving career across music, films and the theatre. Among the many clips are performances for Top of the Pops, The Old Grey Whistle Test and Later with Jools Holland, plus Bowie in interviews about his roles in The Elephant Man, Merry Christmas Mr Lawrence and Labyrinth

programme.title: Internal Affairs (1990)
programme.desc: A crooked cop uses his badge and uniform as a cover for his unsavoury money-making schemes and is prepared to murder anyone who stands in his way. A zealous investigator vows to bring him to justice, but the cunning detective responds by turning his adversary's wife against him. Crime thriller, starring Richard Gere, Andy Garcia, Laurie Metcalf, Nancy Travis and William Baldwin

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler plants root crops and leafy vegetables in her urban garden, including kale, Swiss chard and beetroot, before preparing dishes of rhubarb and raspberry pie, and beetroot and yoghurt soup. She also collects a good supply of eggs from her two chickens

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to the Wiltshire market town of Melksham, as he meets a group who have entered the annual flower competition for five years, but never won gold

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don give a masterclass on growing Mediterranean herbs, plants lily bulbs for summer scent, and in the vegetable garden, he begins to sow seeds for salad root crops

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in the Nidderdale Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in North Yorkshire, helping prepare for the return of both animals and visitors to the hills. She joins a team keeping track of adders emerging from hibernation to find out why numbers are falling. On his Cotswold farm, Adam Henson's struggling with the unpredictable spring weather, and Tom Heap's investigates how a rise in pet ownership is fuelling dog thefts. Plus, wildlife film-maker Jack Perks celebrates the grayling

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond walks the Postie's Path on the Coigach peninsula in Wester Ross, finding out how this coastal route transformed life in this remote community and how it's being protected for the future. Euan McIlwraith heads to Perthshire to find out why a flock of sheep is being let loose to graze a field of wheat and celebrate one of his favourite birds - the Kingfisher. With the World Clydesdale Show coming to Scotland next year, Arlene Stuart meets the mares and their newly born foals hoping to take part, while Anne Lundon searches for Feroux trout in the depths of Loch Ness

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray set about fixing, feeding and repairing the lawns, while Kirsty Wilson creates a new living lawn and Brian Cunningham encourages people not to mow if possible. Chris Beardshaw has updates on the projects he started in his home garden last year

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef goes in search of the best chicken korma in Lucknow, learns the complexities of making rice dish pulao and joins thousands of partygoers at one of India's major Hindu festivals. Further north in Punjab, he observes the age-old process involved in the production of the unrefined sugar known as jaggery and is recruited as a guest judge to find the state's new 'Star Chef'. Rick also demonstrates how to make nimish, a dessert with hints of saffron, pistachios and cardamom, as well as vegetarian curry paneer jalfrezi

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They serve the panel of judges, and one of Britain's best known designers, Thomas Heatherwick. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first eight frames of the match take place. Ronnie O'Sullivan took early control over Kyren Wilson in last year's corresponding session taking a 6-2 lead and recording the first century of the match in the seventh frame. While the length of this match means that neither of the players will feel victory is beyond them even if they find themselves with a similar deficit, the opening session sets the scene for the rest of the encounter, and should also help to settle the nerves that can affect even the most seasoned of professionals at the home of snooker

programme.title: Down the Mighty River with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part programme, the naturalist travels down the Baliem River's lower gorge, one of the most dangerous stretches of white water in the world surrounded by jagged rock walls. His exploration of the surrounding jungle leads to an encounter with a tribe that has had little contact with the outside world. As he concludes his trek in the river's coastal reaches, he faces a crisis of conscience as he witnesses a traditional crocodile hunt

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where nine frames are scheduled to take place. Kyren Wilson enjoyed his best session of last year's showpiece match against Ronnie O'Sullivan at this stage, reducing the deficit by a frame to trail 10-7 overnight and give himself a chance of claiming the world title for the first time in his career. The Warrior defeated Gary Wilson in the first round this year, while the Rocket eased through against Michael Holt at the top of the draw, although that section was littered with former champions, including the likes of John Higgins, Mark Williams and Mark Selby

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: The comedian and explorer revisits his fifth travel series, an epic journey across the world's highest mountain range that was the fulfilment of a boyhood dream inspired by the 1953 ascent of Everest by Hillary and Tensing. Beginning in the famous Khyber Pass, he travels through Pakistan and India, ascends into Nepal and Tibet to reach Everest base camp itself

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar calls on Matt, and Peter warns Emma that Samuel is not what he seems. Noah enlists Lauren and Suresh to help him destroy the power of the carnival, while Claire tries to prevent a bloodbath. Starring Jack Coleman, Zachary Quinto and Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claire is taken aback to learn the dark secrets of Noah's past. Lauren takes decisive action to thwart Samuel, and Peter tries to save Sylar, who has been imprisoned in his own worst nightmare

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A conflicted Hanssen worries that the upcoming court case will cause more harm than good. Does his abuser still hold power over him? Ange's hopes of a quiet night shift are shattered when Dom and Sacha behave suspiciously and Fletch is daunted as he gets to grip with the hospital's disastrous finances

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Extensive highlights of the semi-final, which was filmed just days before the first UK-wide lockdown back in March last year. The semi-final sees the winners of the keyboard, strings, brass, woodwind and percussion finals compete for the three coveted places in the grand final. Organist and conductor Anna Lapwood is joined by Tim Rhys-Evans - director of music at the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama who provides insight and analysis. Josie D'Arby also joins the line-up with interviews with the semi-finalists and Jess Gillam continues her series of conversations discussing the changing face of classical music with conductor Nicholas Collon

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Further coverage of the evening session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the third session of the first semi-final takes place

programme.title: Ronnie's: Ronnie Scott & His World-Famous Jazz Club
programme.desc: The history of Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club, eponymously named after the late tenor saxophonist who founded the establishment with business partner Pete King. It opened in London's Soho in October 1959, inspired by the vibrant post-war jazz venues in New York. Since then, Ronnie's has consistently played host to many of the world's greatest jazz legends, including Chet Baker, Count Basie, Miles Davies, Ella Fitzgerald, Buddy Rich and Nina Simone who are all featured in the film. Contributors include Ronnie's friends, family and the music elite, including Chris Blackwell, Quincy Jones and Sonny Rollins

programme.title: Jazz 625 Live: For One Night Only
programme.desc: A one-off revival of the iconic 1960s music show. Broadcasting live from Cheltenham during the Jazz Festival, the special show will feature a house band - led by pianist Robert Mitchell - with special guests including Gregory Porter, Joshua Redman, Jacqui Dankworth and Cleo Laine. Plus, an exclusive recorded performance from Rolling Stones drummer Charlie Watts in a jazz quartet with saxophonist Scott Hamilton. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts how the first Venetians inhabited the lagoon in the fifth century AD and created an almost impregnable location, which took the shape of the Venice known today through pioneering engineering work. He reveals the stunning interiors of the Doge's Palace, the Basilica of St Mark and the Ca da Mosto, and uncovers a secret history - beset by violence and political intrigue

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present the ultimate guide to the Cornish coast, from the River Tamar to Tintagel Castle, as they discover what makes the stretch so unique. As well as selecting some of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years, Tessa takes a ride on a variety of boats to learn the untold secrets of the Cornish shores. The presenters go line fishing with a local Looe fisherman, explore serpentine rock on The Lizard with a leading geologist, uncover a story of tragedy at sea, and find out what it is like to live in the storm-hit harbour of Porthleven

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Danish detective drama in which each episode covers one day of an investigation. Copenhagen police inspector Sarah Lund says goodbye to her colleagues in the city's homicide department as she prepares to move to Sweden - but is forced to reconsider her plans when a teenage girl goes missing. Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen star. In Danish, German and English

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: The body of 19-year-old girl Nanna Birk Larsen is discovered, and Lund decides to postpone her move to Sweden to lead the murder investigation. Mayoral candidate Troels Hartmann is implicated in the killing when it emerges the car in which the corpse was found was registered to his campaign office - but the driver of the vehicle needs to be identified. Danish drama, starring Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen. In Danish, German and English

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and her intended successor Jan Meyer follow the trail of the prime murder suspect, but her impulsive nature threatens the investigation. Meanwhile, important new clues emerge at the victim's school and confidential information is leaked from the campaign office of mayoral candidate Troels Hartmann. Danish drama, starring Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen. In Danish, German and English

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Comedy about Julia, a middle-class mother who moves out of her comfort zone and reluctantly invites the entire class to her kid's birthday party. programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: A school fundraiser is not Julia's idea of an evening out but somehow she finds herself heavily involved in order to impress a former colleague and new mum crush. Meanwhile, Kevin has a battle with the cloakroom and despite Anne's protests, and Liz takes care of the bar. Comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: A pool party ruins Julia's plans before an important event at work. Liz is after a date, and a new Dad on the scene who goes way back with Amanda, puts Kevin's nose out of joint. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: While Paul's away he has the perfect solution to help out Julia - he sends his parents to help. Meanwhile, Amanda is having a clearout and reluctantly gives Liz her old coat, which seems to have an effect on Liz. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Anne's new car propels her into the limelight as the mums try to get in on her new car pool, while Kevin finds himself in the new role of confidant as Amanda shares a deep secret. Liz and Julia's friendship is under fire as Liz realises she is just a 'mum' friend

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia's childcare problems are solved after she finds the perfect nanny, but her friendship with Liz is strained. Elsewhere, the school caretaker is in hospital, but the mums have lost their ring leader and there's nobody to organise a card. Comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present the ultimate guide to the Cornish coast, from the River Tamar to Tintagel Castle, as they discover what makes the stretch so unique. The presenters go line fishing with a local Looe fisherman, explore serpentine rock on The Lizard with a leading geologist, uncover a story of tragedy at sea, and find out what it is like to live in the storm-hit harbour of Porthleven

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Malaika is a wildlife film-maker and conservationist. Growing up in India inspired her to want to save the wildlife on her doorstep. She has always been fascinated by elephants and her mission is to investigate why these beautiful animals are is in such danger. The Indian elephant is classified as endangered, and Malaika learns that the biggest threats they face are forest fragmentation and living in captivity. She goes undercover to find out more about elephants in the tourist trade in Indian cities and then visits an elephant hospital run by Wildlife SOS, a charity which rehabilitates elephants rescued from captivity. Some of the elephants here were in chains for over 50 years and now have a chance to enjoy life in the wild. Malaika talks about the importance of being aware of wildlife tourism and encourages us to say no to exploitative elephant rides available on holiday.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, River, Araiya and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag are numbered building blocks, and all the parents and little ones have different objects and toys that are themed around numbers. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate number-themed objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The traditional children’s nursery rhyme One, Two, Buckle My Shoe features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring numbers too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.desc: Join 19-month-old Jac and Jemima as they discover the world around them and start to learn how to care for themselves. Jac brushes his hair as he gets ready for the day, and Jemima chooses what she wants to wear. Once ready, Jac’s family visits a local farm shop where they choose a plant and get to pick vegetables! Meanwhile, Jemima goes to a play cafe, where she enjoys a tea party with mum and dad. Back at home, Jac and dad plant their new greenery and talk about how it’ll grow big and tall, just like Jac! Jemima finishes the day with painting flowers using her hands, but she must remember to wash them afterwards! Jac has his very first haircut - what will he think? Finally, after learning how to do so many new things, they both get ready for bed. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is relaxing at home, playing games and watching television. She shows us how a television screen works using millions of tiny squares called pixels. Using her special microscope camera, Maddie shows us how a pixel can make any colour from just three coloured lights - red, blue and green. Next she visits a factory where carpet is made. Maddie follows the journey that wool takes, from shearing a sheep to going through the factory, to end up in the carpets we have at home. Maddie uses her special slow-motion camera to show us how the different coloured threads are woven together on a loom to make a carpet.
