programme.desc: A scaly little visitor gives Nick and Norman an idea for today's Popcast Song

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Robert brings four dusters to life to help dust and tidy around Justin's House. However, the mischievous Dusterlings soon go rogue, making a mess and tickling anyone that moves

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Jen accidentally destroys a video of an Arctic fox hunting, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile and heads to the Arctic in order to re-record the action on his gizmo

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers have fun on a walk to play on the beach!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon recalls the first time he met Waffle as a pup and the pair share memories of the Wonder Dog becoming part of the Brooklyn-Bell family

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: The duo cannot wait to find out what is inside the box that they have taken care of for Mr Fishwick

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Okai has invited his friends over for a traditional Ghanaian meal made using okra. He works very hard in the kitchen to make a lovely tasting, colourful stew for them to enjoy

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: A wrong bus later and the pair are back where they started! It's time to look at the schedule and see when their bus is due to arrive.

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack and Salty visit Topsy Turvy Bay, where everything is upside down, downside up and totally topsy turvy

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley finds out about radishes. JB Gill goes to market with a farming family to buy a new ewe and lambs, then goes in search of some baby animals

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee cleans his biscuit tin collection. The squirrels, inspired to make their own collections, display their precious things in the clubhouse

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are sitting on the sofa after a busy day. Suddenly, there is a power cut. The squirrels cannot see anything, but fortunately Duggee has his torch

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are watching for polar bears, but an iceberg is heading for their ship

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Purl have started the Amazing Owl Club, with its own rulebook, a members-only clubhouse and - best of all - a special badge with flapping owl wings

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The sleepwalking Snowy Snooze is so fast asleep that it walks straight into B.O.T.! The sleepy beastie shuffles onwards through the snow and B.O.T. tries to keep out of its way whilst still collecting the data for a successful mission.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend become animal keepers at an animal park. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble looks after an animal too - his friend, Spotty the horse

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Bishop Rose introduces us to her majestic Mitre that she wears when she leads services in the Church of England.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb thinks a mop head is a living animal and adopts it as a new pet

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug tries to remember an old nursery rhyme. He and Dog go to a fair where they meet the cat and the fiddle, the cow that jumps over the moon and the dish that ran away with the spoon. Dog puts all the pieces together to make a great nursery rhyme.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran have their own sports day, including an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Dad takes the twins to the dentist for a check-up and, after hearing about the tooth fairy from their friend Josie, Topsy comes up with a plan to catch a glimpse of her

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: While Zoe helps Doug with his homework project, about Alaska, Simon and Jess take Waffle and Evie to the shops to buy new school shoes. However, Waffle causes chaos

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Zola-Rose discovers lots of interesting facts about super-slow snails! Rory and his son Sandy search for snails in the garden and put on a very exciting snail race, while Ferne meets some very rare snails and holds a Giant African Land Snail at the zoo. Plus, we find out why snails are so important to the environment. They play an important part in a giant food web.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Magic is in the air as Mum has a wizarding surprise waiting for Iona and Elba in the garden. Later, the whole family head out to launch their kite in the windy sky - but will it take flight?

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas of activities to do with your little one using technology. Learn how video calls are a great way of keeping in touch with family and friends, who might not live nearby. Join in with the Toddler Club Bop! Hosted by Giovanna from her own home.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry refuses to admit he has been playing with Kari's building blocks, but as he tells more and more lies, he realises bending the truth can result in people getting hurt

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour silver, like silver-looking zips, and explore objects that appear silver-coloured in towns and cities. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour silver.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of trees to see how we can help look after them and our planet. Discover how trees grow, how we can tell the age of trees and how the roots, leaves and bark work. Maddie shows how trees are home to lots of other plants, animals, birds and insects who live on and amongst them and have an important role in our ecosystem. We’ll discover a tree’s leaves make energy using sunlight, so they can keep growing. We learn that a tree is alive, it drinks and breathes and grows just like we do! And we’ll find out about deciduous trees that lose their leaves in winter and evergreens that keep their leaves all year round.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Professor Muddles’ misfiring invention causes him and Keith to swap bodies.

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The little girl accompanies Mr McColl to visit her Uncle Matthew, who lives over on the other side of the Island and is a bit of a hermit. She is delighted, because she's been feeling rather grumpy towards her little brother Liam and is rather curious to see her uncle

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah decides to bake a cake to celebrate Duck's birthday

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Charlie wins first prize at school for his home-made rocket, which he made from 10 yoghurt pots and some extra wide tin foil. As he puts his rocket safely away on the top shelf he explains to Lola that the rocket really isn't a toy and she must promise not to touch it or play with it. Then Lola and her imaginary friend, Soren Lorenson, find that they have to transport an elephant back to the jungle, so Soren persuades Lola that using the rocket might be a good idea. Needless to say, the duo soon break the treasured possession

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small find a box that has fallen from space. It contains just two circles of black cloth which are holes, and one leads to another. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle loses his blanket and it turns out he has dropped it on the Pontipine house

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: JB Gill reads Families, Families, Families! by Suzanne and Max Lang

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit properties in Peckham, south-east London, Bilston near Wolverhampton, and a flat in Maidstone

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood lifts the lid on what really happens to Britain's waste, finding out how non-recyclable rubbish is turned into energy to power homes and businesses

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Birmingham, a buyer who was born on the weekend of the first moon landings seeks to mark her 50th year with something 'out of this world'

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper. Before the reds and blues' chosen items go under the hammer, Charles Hanson auctions off a rare Chinese treasure that was discovered inside a carrier bag on its way to a charity shop

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph is at the Walsall Recycling Centre, where she digs out a bamboo chair uncovered from some garden waste, an unwanted pine corner unit, and a mid-century bedside table

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher is thrilled to find himself working on a remarkable, quirky train set designed and built by celebrated cartoonist and innovator Rowland Emmet, the man behind the inventions in Chitty Chitty Bang Bang. A sailor's trinket box and a doll also receive some much-needed attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the working week, introducing the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mitch gets a shock when he goes to wake Bailey. Keegan and Tiffany are nervous ahead of their meeting with the bank manager. Honey threatens Billy that she will cancel Janet's photo shoot if he does not resolve her pay issues with Estelle

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong guest hosts, inviting comedian Joe Lycett and journalist Kirsty Wark to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now the main man on BBC magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer. However, conscious of being left in the shadow of co-presenter Jennie and alert to potential changes behind the scenes, he grows ever more anxious about his place in the big time. Return of the media-based comedy, starring Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: A celebration of the entrepreneurial spirit among the young. This opening episode features Nanial Jones, who invested all his savings into a vintage clothing business. Plus, Alaw Haf who now makes £5,000 a month from subscriptions on the OnlyFans website, having lost her gym job in lockdown, and Toni Leigh Hall who has launched a new line of humorous cards, calendars, door-mats, mouse-mats and face masks

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring appearances by Tom Cruise, Rebel Wilson, Rege-Jean Page, Rosamund Pike, Tom Jones, Keeley Hawes, Ian Wright, Sienna Miller, Sam Neill, Kate Winslet, Alan Carr, Felicity Kendal, Adrian Lester, Amy Poehler, Daniel Kaluuya, Jennifer Garner, Stanley Tucci, Cush Jumbo, Liam Neeson, Octavia Spencer, Melissa McCarthy and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Blair Witch Project (1999)
programme.desc: Three film students head into the American backwoods to make a documentary about the fabled Blair Witch. There proves to be more to the urban legend than meets the eye, and as night falls they find themselves in very real danger. Horror, presented as camcorder footage shot by the characters during their investigation, starring Heather Donahue, Michael Williams and Joshua Leonard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Tom Brown and Sally Abe, and introduces highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Former Great British Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain is in East Anglia as she continues her culinary road trip around Britain, going out to sea with Norfolk fisherman Richard Matthews to catch crabs for Vietnamese-style summer rolls. She then heads to Ely in Cambridgeshire to meet potato farmer and crisp factory owner Ross Taylor, using his snacks to make a crisp, chocolate and salted peanut dessert. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a ploughman's cheese and pickle tart, and a herby chicken and potato salad

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Liverpool

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Coverage of the fourth and concluding session of the opening semi-final, where the first player to reach 17 frames will progress. Kyren Wilson booked his place in the final last year in the most dramatic of circumstances, with the deciding frame lasting 62 minutes and breaking the record for the most combined points scored in a single frame at the Crucible, with Wilson eventually winning it 103-83

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Debbie McGee, Ben Cajee, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are Dame Margot Fonteyn, West Ham United, Inside No.9, and the women of Imperial Rome. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as writers and illustrators Steve Cavanagh, Paul Tonkinson, Emma Kennedy, Jen Brister, Kate Mosse, Adele Parks, Chris Riddell, and Lemn Sissay take part in the general knowledge quiz. The contestants try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are a father and daughter from Northamptonshire, and couples from Lancashire and Rotherham. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Fleur East, as they assist two friends from London in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing acrobat, a princess and a karate champion

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan resolves to confess her drug smuggling to the police. Terrified that Jan is about to throw her life away, Iain contacts Ross in prison and urges him to step in to save her. Jacob is determined to save a woman who has fallen through a skylight, but feelings run high concerning the patient when she is revealed to have a white supremacist tattoo. Feeling dejected at missing out on the paramedic position, Leon decides to leave Holby

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose tries to force Faith into helping her with the repossession of Cindy's salon, unaware that her daughter is working with the police to gather evidence against her. Her campaign of revenge takes an even more personal turn when she kidnaps Alys and Megan from school and reveals their mother's darkest secret. Osian falls into a coma, and the doctors do not expect him to recover, but Faith refuses to give up on him and insists on pressing ahead with the court hearing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Fulham at Craven Cottage. The Citizens will have been looking to emulate their 4-0 victory over Palace in January's reverse fixture and move a step closer to the title, while added spice was added to the London derby due to Chelsea needing the points to boost their top four hopes and Fulham battling for survival. Plus, Everton v Aston Villa and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leeds United

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most popular cult heroes in Premier League history

programme.title: Flying Blind (2012)
programme.desc: An aerospace engineer designing military drones begins a passionate affair with a younger Muslim student. The relationship leads to her being questioned by the security services, who claim he is involved in terrorism, forcing her to question how much she knows about her lover and where her loyalties lie. Drama, showing in tribute to the late Helen McCrory. With Najib Oudghiri and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Fulham at Craven Cottage. The Citizens will have been looking to emulate their 4-0 victory over Palace in January's reverse fixture and move a step closer to the title, while added spice was added to the London derby due to Chelsea needing the points to boost their top four hopes and Fulham battling for survival. Plus, Everton v Aston Villa and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leeds United

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley discusses the importance of wellbeing, meeting therapy dogs that help children learn to read and visiting the UK's first Muslim Ballet School

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: Seven celebrities don their backpacks to find out whether a medieval pilgrimage still has relevance today, taking on a 780km trek across northern Spain over 15 days. Actor Neil Morrissey, entertainer Debbie McGee, comedian Ed Byrne, singer Heather Small, the Rev Kate Bottley, journalist Raphael Rowe and TV presenter JJ Chalmers start by walking the most gruelling part of the Camino, a 26km hike, following a mountain pass from France into Spain that takes them to a height of 1,400m. Each day ends with a stay in a basic hostel, sleeping in dormitories on bunkbeds. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum and Sean Fletcher mark the 100th anniversary of Northern Ireland, meeting Christians from all denominations. Featuring a special performance by Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Bill (2015)
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is determined to become a successful playwright after getting kicked out of a folk band. Heading to London to seek his fortune, he gets caught up in a plot by the king of Spain to blow up Elizabeth I. Historical comedy with the cast of Horrible Histories, starring Mathew Baynton and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: When Easter weekend arrives, the Jones and Braddock families look forward to a welcome break, but tempers fray as their expectations clash with the realities of Victorian life. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen presents a selection of small-screen clips celebrating community spirit, a world of jalopy racing, bucking broncos and local heroes from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven celebrates 50 years as a BBC presenter and his 1250th episode of Countryfile by revisiting parts of the country that hold special memories for him. He uncovers hidden treasures in his childhood Yorkshire home, learns about peasant poet John Clare in Cambridgeshire, and visits an ancient Scottish battlefield

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts a special episode reflecting on the aftermath of the Second World War filmed at Coventry Cathedral, in which people bring in items that date back to the end of the conflict and reveal the personal stories behind them. Featuring a ballgown and a set of handmade playing cards that offer a glimpse into the final days of the war in the Far East, a fragile teacup that survived the bombing of Dresden, and a scrapbook documenting life in post-war Berlin

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Sister Hilda are both bewildered and concerned by a patient's unexplained symptoms, and try to get to the bottom of her ill health before it impacts her pregnancy. A woman expecting her third child is worried about her recent weight gain and turns to Lucille for support, leading to an unexpected discovery

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: With time running out, AC-12 attempt to unmask H, the fourth individual commanding the network of corrupt officers behind the murder of Gail Vella. However, sinister and powerful forces appear intent on orchestrating a cover-up. Steve Oram (Sightseers) guest stars in the final episode of the current series

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Liverpool at Old Trafford. Liverpool had not won here since 2014 and United had already recorded a home win against them in the FA Cup this term, but the last two league meetings at this venue had ended in draws. Plus, Newcastle United v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Bristol City v Manchester United and Everton v Arsenal

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Four people claim to be an artist called Erica, living with partner Sam in a stylish property in south-east London. Panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix and guest Katie Piper are tasked with determining which is the true Erica, as each of the quartet airs their opinion on the real Erica's taste in fashion, her passion for Sam, the story of their wedding day and the name of their plumber. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.desc: Former Great British Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain is in East Anglia as she continues her culinary road trip around Britain, going out to sea with Norfolk fisherman Richard Matthews to catch crabs for Vietnamese-style summer rolls. She then heads to Ely in Cambridgeshire to meet potato farmer and crisp factory owner Ross Taylor, using his snacks to make a crisp, chocolate and salted peanut dessert. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a ploughman's cheese and pickle tart, and a herby chicken and potato salad

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Liverpool

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Mark Selby. Coverage of the fourth and concluding session of the opening semi-final, where the first player to reach 17 frames will progress. Kyren Wilson booked his place in the final last year in the most dramatic of circumstances, with the deciding frame lasting 62 minutes and breaking the record for the most combined points scored in a single frame at the Crucible, with Wilson eventually winning it 103-83

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Debbie McGee, Ben Cajee, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are Dame Margot Fonteyn, West Ham United, Inside No.9, and the women of Imperial Rome. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as writers and illustrators Steve Cavanagh, Paul Tonkinson, Emma Kennedy, Jen Brister, Kate Mosse, Adele Parks, Chris Riddell, and Lemn Sissay take part in the general knowledge quiz. The contestants try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are a father and daughter from Northamptonshire, and couples from Lancashire and Rotherham. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Fleur East, as they assist two friends from London in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing acrobat, a princess and a karate champion

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan resolves to confess her drug smuggling to the police. Terrified that Jan is about to throw her life away, Iain contacts Ross in prison and urges him to step in to save her. Jacob is determined to save a woman who has fallen through a skylight, but feelings run high concerning the patient when she is revealed to have a white supremacist tattoo. Feeling dejected at missing out on the paramedic position, Leon decides to leave Holby

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose tries to force Faith into helping her with the repossession of Cindy's salon, unaware that her daughter is working with the police to gather evidence against her. Her campaign of revenge takes an even more personal turn when she kidnaps Alys and Megan from school and reveals their mother's darkest secret. Osian falls into a coma, and the doctors do not expect him to recover, but Faith refuses to give up on him and insists on pressing ahead with the court hearing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Fulham at Craven Cottage. The Citizens will have been looking to emulate their 4-0 victory over Palace in January's reverse fixture and move a step closer to the title, while added spice was added to the London derby due to Chelsea needing the points to boost their top four hopes and Fulham battling for survival. Plus, Everton v Aston Villa and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leeds United

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Livingston v Aberdeen, Motherwell v Kilmarnock and Hibernian v St Johnstone

programme.title: Flying Blind (2012)
programme.desc: An aerospace engineer designing military drones begins a passionate affair with a younger Muslim student. The relationship leads to her being questioned by the security services, who claim he is involved in terrorism, forcing her to question how much she knows about her lover and where her loyalties lie. Drama, showing in tribute to the late Helen McCrory. With Najib Oudghiri and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Fulham at Craven Cottage. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley discusses the importance of wellbeing, meeting therapy dogs that help children learn to read and visiting the UK's first Muslim Ballet School

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: David Strachan talks to Rabbi Moshe Rubin, Scotland's Senior Rabbi, from Giffnock and Newlands Synagogue, about the experience of Passover

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss offer their advice as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Lincoln Antiques Fair, prior to auction in Grantham. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross asks whether silver has lost its shine

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum and Sean Fletcher mark the 100th anniversary of Northern Ireland, meeting Christians from all denominations. Featuring a special performance by Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following five of the world's most endangered species as they struggle to preserve their social groups, beginning with a chimpanzee troop in Senegal. As the dry season sets in, the Alpha of the pack, who has ruled unchallenged for the past three years, faces a series of challenges and finds little support from the other apes

programme.title: Bill (2015)
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is determined to become a successful playwright after getting kicked out of a folk band. Heading to London to seek his fortune, he gets caught up in a plot by the king of Spain to blow up Elizabeth I. Historical comedy with the cast of Horrible Histories, starring Mathew Baynton and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Reports on rural and agricultural developments around the country

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven celebrates 50 years as a BBC presenter and his 1250th episode of Countryfile by revisiting parts of the country that hold special memories for him. He uncovers hidden treasures in his childhood Yorkshire home, learns about peasant poet John Clare in Cambridgeshire, and visits an ancient Scottish battlefield

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts a special episode reflecting on the aftermath of the Second World War filmed at Coventry Cathedral, in which people bring in items that date back to the end of the conflict and reveal the personal stories behind them. Featuring a ballgown and a set of handmade playing cards that offer a glimpse into the final days of the war in the Far East, a fragile teacup that survived the bombing of Dresden, and a scrapbook documenting life in post-war Berlin

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Sister Hilda are both bewildered and concerned by a patient's unexplained symptoms, and try to get to the bottom of her ill health before it impacts her pregnancy. A woman expecting her third child is worried about her recent weight gain and turns to Lucille for support, leading to an unexpected discovery

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: With time running out, AC-12 attempt to unmask H, the fourth individual commanding the network of corrupt officers behind the murder of Gail Vella. However, sinister and powerful forces appear intent on orchestrating a cover-up. Steve Oram (Sightseers) guest stars in the final episode of the current series

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Liverpool at Old Trafford. Liverpool had not won here since 2014 and United had already recorded a home win against them in the FA Cup this term, but the last two league meetings at this venue had ended in draws. Plus, Newcastle United v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Rangers v Celtic. Steven Thompson presents action from the Old Firm derby in the Premiership, held at Ibrox. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Bristol City v Manchester United and Everton v Arsenal

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit properties in Peckham, south-east London, Bilston near Wolverhampton, and a flat in Maidstone

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood lifts the lid on what really happens to Britain's waste, finding out how non-recyclable rubbish is turned into energy to power homes and businesses

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper. Before the reds and blues' chosen items go under the hammer, Charles Hanson auctions off a rare Chinese treasure that was discovered inside a carrier bag on its way to a charity shop

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Birmingham, a buyer who was born on the weekend of the first moon landings seeks to mark her 50th year with something 'out of this world'

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Shropshire and Herefordshire to help a recently retired couple with a £550,000 budget find a dream country home that is full of traditional period charm. Nicki also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph is at the Walsall Recycling Centre, where she digs out a bamboo chair uncovered from some garden waste, an unwanted pine corner unit, and a mid-century bedside table

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher is thrilled to find himself working on a remarkable, quirky train set designed and built by celebrated cartoonist and innovator Rowland Emmet, the man behind the inventions in Chitty Chitty Bang Bang. A sailor's trinket box and a doll also receive some much-needed attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the working week, introducing the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mitch gets a shock when he goes to wake Bailey. Keegan and Tiffany are nervous ahead of their meeting with the bank manager. Honey threatens Billy that she will cancel Janet's photo shoot if he does not resolve her pay issues with Estelle

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong guest hosts, inviting comedian Joe Lycett and journalist Kirsty Wark to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now the main man on BBC magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer. However, conscious of being left in the shadow of co-presenter Jennie and alert to potential changes behind the scenes, he grows ever more anxious about his place in the big time. Return of the media-based comedy, starring Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Colin Murphy, Diona Doherty and Neil Delamere satirise and lampoon the people and events which have been making the news in Northern Ireland and further afield. Alongside comedian and quiz expert Paul Sinha, the team fields questions from a virtual audience who will undoubtedly want them to wag the finger of blame at someone. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring appearances by Tom Cruise, Rebel Wilson, Rege-Jean Page, Rosamund Pike, Tom Jones, Keeley Hawes, Ian Wright, Sienna Miller, Sam Neill, Kate Winslet, Alan Carr, Felicity Kendal, Adrian Lester, Amy Poehler, Daniel Kaluuya, Jennifer Garner, Stanley Tucci, Cush Jumbo, Liam Neeson, Octavia Spencer, Melissa McCarthy and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Blair Witch Project (1999)
programme.desc: Three film students head into the American backwoods to make a documentary about the fabled Blair Witch. There proves to be more to the urban legend than meets the eye, and as night falls they find themselves in very real danger. Horror, presented as camcorder footage shot by the characters during their investigation, starring Heather Donahue, Michael Williams and Joshua Leonard

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: Seven celebrities don their backpacks to find out whether a medieval pilgrimage still has relevance today, taking on a 780km trek across northern Spain over 15 days. Actor Neil Morrissey, entertainer Debbie McGee, comedian Ed Byrne, singer Heather Small, the Rev Kate Bottley, journalist Raphael Rowe and TV presenter JJ Chalmers start by walking the most gruelling part of the Camino, a 26km hike, following a mountain pass from France into Spain that takes them to a height of 1,400m. Each day ends with a stay in a basic hostel, sleeping in dormitories on bunkbeds. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies reports on the excitement at Union Station in Los Angeles, which is co-hosting this year's Academy Awards. Several big name stars tell the programme team how they view the 2021 Oscars contest, which has the most diverse field of contenders ever. British actor Riz Ahmed discusses the significance of becoming the first Muslim to be nominated for a best actor Oscar. Also, celebrated Danish film-maker Thomas Vinterberg discusses his drinking drama Another Round, favoured to win for best international feature film. The programme will also reveal what millions of viewers around the world can expect to see when they tune in to watch this in-person Oscars ceremony, which has been significantly reinvented because of the global pandemic.

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.desc: The hapless bumbler attempts to make his own home movie, a horror thriller with Mrs Wicket in the starring role. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler has to look after Mrs Wicket's goldfish, but Scrapper the cat is keen to help. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben and Gwen learn a rumor of an alien invasion happening at the park they are camping at but when they investigate they discover a preserve containing a host of mutated creatures engineered by Animo! With the unexpected help of Kevin 11 and his family Ben proves that the rumors are false and that truth is stranger than fiction!

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Comedian Rosie Jones and actress Catherine Tyldesley join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by Radio One's Jordan North and Game of Talents host Vernon Kay, and there are recipes for fish and chips, raspberry tart, and toad-in-the-hole. Guest chefs Brian Turner and Dipna Anand also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef prepares a selection of dishes perfect for al fresco dining, including Cypriot lamb chops, cherry tomato skewers and potato salad. Supper Club host Joseph Denison Carey serves up a tagliatelle, and Ainsley shows guest Dev Griffin how to make sesame and singer seabass with an Asian noodles salad. Olly Smith has canned drinks perfect for the great outdoors

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The next leg of James's trip sees him heading to Bresse, home of one of the country's most famous ingredients - the Bresse chicken. He visits a poultry farm to discover just why these birds are so special, and gets the chance to cook his own. Moving on, he meets Georges Blanc, owner of a three-Michelin-starred restaurant - and one of James's culinary heroes

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.40 Qipco 2000 Guineas Stakes from Newmarket, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races at 2.05 from Goodwood and 2.40 from Thirsk. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald and commentary from Richard Hoiles and Brough Scott

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Craig Charles and Rita Simons take part in the knockout tournament, hoping to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. They face a wide range of general knowledge-based rounds under the watchful eye of The Governess, Anne Hegerty, as they attempt to make it through to the semi-finals. Who will succeed and get closer to the chance of winning?

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Rumman, Andrew, Eli and Clair pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Cambridge for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Joel Dommett and Oti Mabuse to try to win big money. They must uncover the secret talents of mystery performers ranging from a speed-eater to a Rubik's cuber, and a Latin dancer to a spider wrangler

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts an updated version of Strike It Lucky, with three pairs trying to navigate their way across a sea of screens, trying to bank prizes and avoid the infamous hotspots along the way. One lucky duo will reach the epic endgame, where they stand to win a mountain of amazing prizes, including a holiday to Mauritius

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Martin Compston, ahead of the hotly anticipated season finale, as well as multiple gold-winning Paralympic swimmer Ellie Simmonds, and Diversity founder and Britain's Got Talent judge Ashley Banjo. Plus, singer-songwriter Paul Weller chats and performs in the studio

programme.title: Fast & Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaw's older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. programme.desc: Anton Du Beke and Lisa Riley join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia meets backpackers in Cairns and heads out to an island turtle sanctuary. She also treks through the oldest rainforest in the world and gets a taste for green ants. Finally, the caretakers of an idyllic island introduce her to the magic of the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh creates a kitchen garden in Oxford for Icolyn Smith, the 88-year-old founder of a charitable foundation dedicated to providing food and support for homeless people, who still cooks and serves food in the soup kitchens she has set up. The team come up with clever design tricks to make the most of long narrow gardens and offer space-saving tips on growing apples

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Twiggy, who pays tribute to the Great British seaside, while horticulturist David Domoney selects garden furniture to help people entertain this summer. Plus, Alan goes behind the scenes at one of the UK's leading farm parks

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef prepares a selection of dishes perfect for al fresco dining, including Cypriot lamb chops, cherry tomato skewers and potato salad. programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.40 Qipco 1000 Guineas Stakes from Newmarket, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races at 2.05 from Salisbury, and 2.40 and 3.15 from Hamilton. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a double-sized edition of digital disasters, featuring videos uploaded from viewers' phones, drones, dashcams and smarthome cameras

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Cambridge for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as comedian Dane Baptiste, rugby referee Nigel Owens, presenter Anita Rani and sports broadcaster Mark Pougatch pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for the charity of their choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as Richard Madeley, Sheila Ferguson and Mark Wright answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Skyfall (2012)
programme.desc: James Bond pursues a mysterious criminal mastermind who has orchestrated a terrorist attack on MI6 headquarters and exposed the identities of undercover agents around the world. The trail leads to a rogue former operative who has a personal vendetta against the intelligence organisation's leader M - who is also facing calls to retire. Sam Mendes directs the 23rd instalment in the spy franchise, starring Daniel Craig as 007, with Javier Bardem, Judi Dench, Ralph Fiennes, Naomie Harris and Ben Whishaw

programme.title: The Millennium Dome Heist with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: The actor reveals the story of the nine-month game of cat-and-mouse between police and a criminal gang intent on stealing diamonds worth £350 million from the Millennium Dome in London in November 2000

programme.title: And Finally - With Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster revisits more quirky news stories from the News at Ten archives, catching up with a man who turned his lounge into a replica of the Starship Enterprise and finding out what became of a girl who was christened with 217 names. There also a selection of irreverent news items from around the world

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia meets backpackers in Cairns and heads out to an island turtle sanctuary. She also treks through the oldest rainforest in the world and gets a taste for green ants. Finally, the caretakers of an idyllic island introduce her to the magic of the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh creates a kitchen garden in Oxford for Icolyn Smith, the 88-year-old founder of a charitable foundation dedicated to providing food and support for homeless people, who still cooks and serves food in the soup kitchens she has set up. The team come up with clever design tricks to make the most of long narrow gardens and offer space-saving tips on growing apples

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Twiggy, who pays tribute to the Great British seaside, while horticulturist David Domoney selects garden furniture to help people entertain this summer. Plus, Alan goes behind the scenes at one of the UK's leading farm parks

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef prepares a selection of dishes perfect for al fresco dining, including Cypriot lamb chops, cherry tomato skewers and potato salad. Supper Club host Joseph Denison Carey serves up a tagliatelle, and Ainsley shows guest Dev Griffin how to make sesame and singer seabass with an Asian noodles salad. Olly Smith has canned drinks perfect for the great outdoors

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Joel Dommett and Oti Mabuse to try to win big money. They must uncover the secret talents of mystery performers ranging from a speed-eater to a Rubik's cuber, and a Latin dancer to a spider wrangler

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.40 Qipco 1000 Guineas Stakes from Newmarket, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races at 2.05 from Salisbury, and 2.40 and 3.15 from Hamilton. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

Sam Mendes directs the 23rd instalment in the spy franchise, starring Daniel Craig as 007, with Javier Bardem, Judi Dench, Ralph Fiennes, Naomie Harris and Ben Whishaw

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish detective's silver wedding celebrations are interrupted by a fresh outbreak of crime on his patch. Five dead bodies have been found on board an abandoned lorry at the ferry terminal and a fugitive is on the run. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A murder victim is found in unusual circumstances, buried with a cache of 100-year-old timber. A likely suspect is soon identified, only to be shot dead by a mystery assassin - a development that has repercussions for DI Lane's love life. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour Cornish copper, with Helen Masters

programme.title: The Suspicions of Mr Whicher
programme.desc: Hired by West Country landowner Sir Henry Coverley, Whicher takes on a seemingly straightforward case of infidelity, following his client's young wife as she meets her lover in London. However, when a key witness mysteriously fails to attend the divorce hearing, the investigation takes a dark twist, leading the private inquiry agent into the heart of the English countryside and to the most disturbing and destructive of secrets. Feature-length Victorian crime drama, starring Paddy Considine, with Helen Bradbury, Risteard Cooper and Alex Robertson

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A college dean is found bludgeoned to death in local woodland, following a heated pub debate. When an alchemic image is discovered by the body, denoting the first of four stages of a process called 'The Magnum Opus', the detectives realise that there are three more murders to follow. Before any more crimes are committed, the team must uncover the guilty secret that the victims share in order to learn the identity of the killer and the potential victims

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an RAF officer is found hanged in a wood, Meredith - the new chief superintendent at Hastings - is quick to dismiss it as a straightforward suicide. However, an aerial photo of a German village found in the dead man's pocket leads Milner to suspect murder. The newly retired Foyle is compelled to return to service when events take an even more unexpected turn. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A psychiatrist's wife is murdered in a seemingly unprovoked attack, and establishing a motive proves harder than catching the killer. Meanwhile, Lane and Kersey disagree at every turn of the investigation. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a woman is found strangled, a pathologist detects chilling similarities with a number of unsolved murders - prompting Wycliffe and his team to reopen 11-year-old case files. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Tim Wylton and Helen Masters

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. His search leads him to a family estate in the Oxfordshire countryside where he meets three siblings divided by personal demons and shared loss. Endeavour suspects there is a link between the disappearance and an unsolved case from years before, but as the investigation continues, conflict erupts between his fellow officers. When a group of teenagers makes a horrifying discovery, he suspects someone is manipulating events from afar

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New receptionist Morwenna Newcross (Jessica Ransom) gets off to a bad start after arriving late on her first day, and her hyperactive behaviour leads Martin to believe she is high on drugs. Guest starring Andrew-Lee Potts (Primeval)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa's hippie mother Eleanor (Louise Jameson) arrives in the village, immediately taking over Martin's kitchen to prepare a herbal tonic, which she later gives to the baby to stop his crying. A culinary rivalry develops between Bert and Mark Bridge, the landlord of the Crab and Lobster pub, at Portwenn Fun Day, with both keen to take advantage of the event by providing food for the spectators

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.desc: Harriet poses as a journalist in an attempt to track a notorious criminal trying to muscle in on London's organised crime scene, and soon becomes embroiled in the machinations of a crafty French jewel thief. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.desc: An Israeli minister is abducted, and the ensuing suburban siege triggers an airborne pursuit as Bodie and Doyle try to save the day. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.desc: The team travel to a venue in Hungary that has produced carp weighing more than 40kg in the past. However, they quickly discover it shares a common problem with many lakes in this part of the world - there are also hordes of hungry smaller carp under 20lb

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. In preparation for an invasion of Wales, King John orders all food from the local community to be collected in order to feed his army. Robin and his band are unwilling to stand by and let their people starve, and form a resistance with the villagers of Wickham

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is called in to help a Russian physicist who wants to defect to America. Vintage adventure, starring Roger Moore, with Guy Deghy, Anthony Booth and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey finds himself the target of a crazed female admirer. Guest starring Elizabeth Sladen and Suzi Quatro

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's girlfriend is critically injured in a restaurant bomb blast, prompting him to seek revenge. When it transpires the intended target was an apparently unimportant accountant, Bodie ignores Cowley's orders and begins probing the man's past. Lewis Collins stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry takes a job at a strip club, while a chance meeting with an old acquaintance gives Arthur an idea to make money out of art forgeries. Vintage comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team head to the Netherlands, which is renowned for its extensive waterways, but they will be focusing their attention on a lake called Carplantis which is home to some fantastic-looking, and very large carp. Plus, they also take in a spot of barbel fishing on the River Meuse

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team film their first-ever episode in the UK, from the Wasing Estate in Berkshire, which has a number of lakes and was previously the home of the British record carp. programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team is given two days and a $20,000 budget to remove a 10-metre steel-hulled yacht from Cockburn Beach in Western Australia. The problem is the boat is buried deep in the sand and is a danger to beach goers. Heavy machinery specialist Ryan is going to use his monster excavator to try and get the job done, but there's a catch - to make the budget work, Luke needs Ryan to be done and dusted in 24 hours.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade returns to Australia's Fitzroy River to investigate one of the world's rarest fish, the glyphis shark - a species so mysterious that very little is known about it. Braving hungry crocodiles and deadly tides, he hopes to become one of the first people to catch this elusive creature

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. In preparation for an invasion of Wales, King John orders all food from the local community to be collected in order to feed his army. Robin and his band are unwilling to stand by and let their people starve, and form a resistance with the villagers of Wickham

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An Israeli minister is abducted, and the ensuing suburban siege triggers an airborne pursuit as Bodie and Doyle try to save the day. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes disguises himself as a plumber to gain access to the home of a blackmailing art dealer who targets members of high society. Robert Hardy guest stars in a feature-length episode of the period drama, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: MacArthur (1977)
programme.desc: Biopic of American general Douglas MacArthur, who earned a celebrated reputation with a series of victories in the Second World War, as well as for his work in rebuilding post-war Japan. However, his controversial approach to the Korean conflict led to his dismissal by President Truman. Starring Gregory Peck, Ed Flanders, Dan O'Herlihy, Sandy Kenyon and Ward Costello

programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty hops over to France to find out what the white skin on Brie is made of. Helen Lawal explores how candy floss is inspiring a food revolution, while Briony May Williams goes crayfish trapping in a bid to learn why it is not possible to buy them whole in the supermarkets

programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Rosie Jones is joined by fellow comedian Jenny Eclair for an 'outdoorsy' adventure on the island of Anglesey in north Wales. The pair discover they'd make terrible farmers when they try their hand at sheep herding and visit the island's many Neolithic stone sites. Rosie takes Jenny on a horse ride along the waterfront, and they take to the skies on the world's fastest zip wire. As the sun sets, they meet a coast craft expert, who teaches them essential survival skills on one of the island's many picturesque beaches - and it's quickly evident they'd not last more than few hours if left to fend for themselves. Narrated by Olivia Colman

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shws including Line of Duty, Snackmasters, Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow, Mastermind, Baby Surgeons: Delivering Miracles, The Art of the Joy of Sex and Dr Pimple Popper. Plus, they observe BBC news coverage of the apparent spat between Dominic Cummings and Boris Johnson

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett in the words-and-numbers game, while Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Ride Along 2 (2016)
programme.desc: Excitable beat cop Ben longs to work alongside his fiancee's brother, hard-boiled detective James, who does not think he has what it takes. James decides to show Ben that he is not cut out for the job by recruiting him to help bring a drug dealer to justice. Comedy sequel, starring Ice Cube, Kevin Hart and Olivia Munn

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: Aine tells Frank that things are getting serious between her and Peter-Brian, prompting him to join forces with Doofus to expose the object of her affection. However, in the process of collecting evidence against him, Frank starts to fall under Peter-Brian's spell - and Doofus worries that Frank has fallen for the 'enemy'. Meanwhile, Mary takes on a tenant or two to help cover the mortgage. Comedy, with Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Newcastle is hosted by mother and workaholic Clare, who aims to impress her guests with an Indian-themed menu of spicy pakoras and lamb curry in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedy promoter David hosts the second night in Newcastle, where he aims to impress his guests with a fine-dining menu featuring caviar, scallops, beef fillet and oysters, in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize. However, some of the guests have reservations about the host, meaning he has his work cut out for him

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third night in Newcastle sees sophisticated marketing consultant Liz trying to impress her guests with a fancy French-themed menu. However, she spends most of her time trying to keep the peace as outspoken comedy promoter David annoys club singer John and rubs mum-of-three Clare up the wrong way with his snobbish views

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Club singer John hopes that a menu inspired by his travels around the world will help him win the £1,000 prize as he hosts the fourth night in Newcastle. He also has a musical surprise in store for his guests, but faces a challenge trying to impress David who refused to take part in the fun and games on Liz's evening. However, further rifts appear in the group when Vicky wants to discuss Geordie icons

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Newcastle and the turn of hotel administrator Vicky to entertain her guests. However, there is tension in the air and the spiritual host is challenged to keep the peace when John lets David know exactly what he thinks of him. The theme of negativity continues as mum-of-three Claire and Liz both take the comedy promoter to task over his questionable sense of humour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is revealed

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Highlights from the first five episodes of the new series in which Kirstie Allsopp offers creative ideas and craft projects for the whole family during the coronavirus lockdown. programme.title: The £100k Drop
