programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: The team acquire a personalised Victorian pistol and try to track down the original owner. A customer brings in brasses that his dad brought back from the Somme

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: A customer brings in a family war letter, describing a man's fear for his life before battle. And animal lover Marie investigates the story of a medal awarded for rescuing a dog

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Dr Tracy Borman, Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, goes behind closed doors to reveal the private lives of six historical figures, beginning with Princess Margaret. Dr Borman discovers the real reason Margaret's relationship with RAF officer Peter Townsend came to an end, uncovers her disastrous marriage to Antony Snowden and discovers more about her love of music and devotion to duty

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a 19th-century schooner in Lake Erie off Ohio's coast that could shed light on not just the founding of Ohio, but the settlement of North America itself

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: Beneath the North Sea lie relics of the Battle of Jutland. It took place during the First World War and an overconfident Royal Navy expected it to be easy

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Poland. He begins in the capital Warsaw, where he takes to the floor to dance to one of Chopin's polonaises with high-school students rehearsing for their leavers' ball, before discovering how the former industrial city of Lodz supplied the vast Russian empire of the early 20th century. He then heads to Poznan and rides one of the few remaining steam-powered commuter trains, visits a factory in Wroclaw that manufactures car bodies for locomotives, and ends his journey in Krakow, where he takes a tour in an iconic vehicle of the communist era

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Senate House in Bloomsbury, Britain's oldest skyscraper and administrative centre of the University of London. Items under valuation include the hoof of a horse that fought in the Battle of Balaclava, a collection of stained glass panels that were recovered from a skip, and a diamond and platinum bracelet

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris embarks on an epic journey through Scandinavia, starting out on the south-west coast of Norway and travelling to the capital Oslo via a blizzard-prone mountain region that doubled as the ice planet Hoth in The Empire Strikes Back. He then heads to Stockholm, where he tours the Swedish city on its newly restored tram system, before crossing into Finland to visit Rovaniemi, known as Santa's home town. Chris also spends a chilly night in an ice hotel and boards a giant iron-ore train bound for Narvik, the final destination of his journey through darkness, ice and snow

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An iron monster lost in the Alaskan tundra, a Crusader fort thrown into a modern conflict, and a prison built to house a mafia kingpin

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A real-life concrete jungle in America's 'Rust Belt', and an industrial facility that brought energy to a remote corner of the world

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: James Ellis visits Italy to delve into the life of Benito Mussolini - the brutal dictator whose violent path to power set the template for the rise of Nazi Germany

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Field Marshal Montgomery's daring airborne plan to end the war by Christmas, which went a bridge too far at Arnhem

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Senate House in Bloomsbury, Britain's oldest skyscraper and administrative centre of the University of London. Items under valuation include the hoof of a horse that fought in the Battle of Balaclava, a collection of stained glass panels that were recovered from a skip, and a diamond and platinum bracelet

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide as he travels through Spain and Portugal. He begins in the city of La Coruna, where he examines the Celtic roots of the Galician people and tries to master the bagpipes, before meeting walkers from all over the world on the pilgrims' trail to Santiago de Compostela. Heading across the border to Porto, Michael finds out about the origins of Britain's long alliance with the Portuguese, and finishes his journey in the capital Lisbon

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: Exploring the history of Westminster Abbey, which dates back to the 11th century and houses more than 500 corpses, including those of Edward I, Elizabeth I, Charles Darwin and Isaac Newton, as well as staging 16 royal weddings. The episode also explores the Houses of Parliament and Big Ben

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: The Napier Railton - the legendary racing car and jewel in the museum's motoring crown - is put through its paces when it meets an old rival

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off a Jensen Interceptor and a Jensen Healey along with a one-off Wood & Pickett Triumph Dolomite Sprint. Sarah takes a shine to a four-tonne road roller

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1971 Mercedes SL and a 1989 Jaguar XJS emerge from a barn on a chicken farm, a Roller and a Rover go under the hammer and a 1966 Triumph Spitfire is restored to near perfection

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in the picturesque village of Thornton-le-Dale

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The InterContinental Shanghai Wonderland, a 337-room hotel that burrows 16 stories below ground level, clinging to the cliffs of an abandoned quarry

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How the Abrams Tank, the US Army's biggest land combat vehicle, is constructed, using access to the factory where they are built, as well as their testing grounds

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: Tim Sutherland, Malin Holst and Simon Richardson explore the site of a siege during the Crusades, where an earthquake preserved the bodies of the soldiers

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: Artefacts related to medieval Italian warlords and their armies of mercenaries, who provided defence for the country's city states which had great wealth but small standing armies

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: The documentary explores the intelligence of marine mammals, discovering just how smart whales and dolphins really are. Scientist Denise Herzing talks about the development of a two-way communication system between dolphins and humans in the Bahamas, while in Western Australia the creatures are filmed using a range of survival skills. In Mexico, grey whales seemingly empathise with humans, while the humpbacks of Alaska pool their talents to increase hunting success. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earth's poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: A polar bear hunts with her cubs, while the warmer weather of spring accelerates a process that will eventually see an area of ice the size of Australia disappear into the Arctic Ocean. A woolly bear caterpillar emerges from the snow after spending the winter frozen, and in Antarctica male Adelie penguins arrive to begin constructing nests in fierce storms as they try to attract mates

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: How animals in the polar regions adapt to the summer weather. In a colony of 400,000 king penguins the adults go surfing and the chicks take mud-baths to cool off, while polar bear cubs take their first swimming lessons as the ice they inhabit melts away. A minke whale is hunted by a family of killer whales, succumbing to their battering after two hours, and Adelie penguins try to prevent their chicks being snatched by skuas

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: With the arrival of autumn the Arctic Ocean begins freezing over, and polar bears gather in large numbers on the coast as they await the return of the ice. Two thousand beluga whales head for a particular estuary to thrash against the gravel and exfoliate, while in Antarctica, Adelie penguins take to the sea for the first time and face the danger of leopard seal attacks

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how the arrival of winter sparks a ruthless battle for survival in the polar regions, with temperatures plummeting to -70C, winds reaching 120mph and day-long darkness. As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. Wolves and bison engage in battle, but alliances are also made, and beneath the snow, voles try to dodge the attacks of great grey owls and weasels

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the life and work of people in the polar regions, including the residents of Norilsk in Siberia, the coldest city on Earth. He also discovers how the Danish armed forces maintain their country's claim to Greenland's natural resources by mounting a 2,000-mile dog sled patrol during the winter, and finds out how a research base at the South Pole is able to cope with being cut off from the outside world for six months at a time

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new railway journey along the south coast of England. Beginning in the port of Dover, he takes a plunge into the English Channel, inspired by the example set by a brave 19th-century sea captain. A miniature steam train one third the size of a conventional locomotive conveys him from Romney Marsh to Dungeness, and in Eastbourne, he learns how the 7th Duke of Devonshire managed to market the town's attractions to the refined upper-crust of Victorian London. The first leg of his trip concludes in eccentric style at the Glyndebourne opera festival on the South Downs

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Newhaven to Worthing, examining the nation's hidden defences, and taking in two magnificent engineering achievements - the Ouse Valley Viaduct and the Clayton Tunnel. High on top of a favourite Victorian beauty spot he learns how trains carried hordes of day-trippers to fly kites, and finds a novel way to pick tomatoes in Worthing

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Littlehampton, where he discovers how Victorian engineers defended the town's residents from cholera. He stops off at Gosport and experiences the lethal firepower unleashed on the French during in an arms race. In the New Forest, the presenter visits Florence Nightingale's family home, before finishing at Beaulieu, where he seizes the opportunity to drive the first motor car

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his latest journey in the sailing town of Lymington, where he makes a life-saving discovery. The route takes him through the Dorchester countryside, where he discovers how the introduction of the railway inspired Thomas Hardy. He tries his hand at carpet-making in Axminster and concludes his journey in Exmouth, where he learns about the work of forgotten Victorian landscape artist Francis Danby

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Plymouth, Michael Portillo finds out about the Royal Navy's fighting spirit and mixes his own blend of 'ruin'. Then, crossing into Cornwall, he learns about the last bridge to be built by renowned engineer Isambard Kingdom Brunel. By Tre, Pol and Pen, the ex-politician comes to know Cornishmen and how to prepare the perfect pasty. This leg of his journey ends in a small village, which in Victorian times became a hub of global communications

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets out from Birmingham on the first leg of a trip to Dartmoor, and a visit to the city's town hall reveals a magnificent organ and the location for a celebrated music festival. Travelling south to Kidderminster, he works in a Royal Mail sorting office and discovers more about great postal innovator Rowland Hill, before he ending his journey in Worcester, where he learns about the origins of the British Medical Association

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates are shocked to discover there is a thief in their midst, so Fletcher decides to stage a mock trial to bring the culprit to justice with the help of Slade's resident legal consultant Judge Rawley. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Maurice Denham

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates decide on a wager as the parole board gathers, with Blanco odds-on favourite for release. However, the old lag turns down his freedom and leaves Fletcher in danger of losing a small fortune. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Porridge

programme.desc: Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall continue their quest to curry favour with omnipotent Taskmaster Greg Davies. Greg's assistant Alex Horne takes notes, records times and offers the contestants some much-needed moral support

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson meets 21-year-old YouTuber Jay from London, who has no problem messaging women online but cannot make connections in real life, and 28-year-old Victoria from Liverpool, who is looking for her very own Xena, Warrior Princess. Will the back-to-basics approach of Naked Attraction help either of them find what they're looking for?

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill, actor-presenter-musician Martin Kemp, comedian and actress Roisin Conaty and broadcaster Bill Turnbull take part in the charity contest. Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted challenges, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills

programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Rosie Jones is joined by fellow comedian Jenny Eclair for an 'outdoorsy' adventure on the island of Anglesey in north Wales. The pair discover they'd make terrible farmers when they try their hand at sheep herding and visit the island's many Neolithic stone sites. Rosie takes Jenny on a horse ride along the waterfront, and they take to the skies on the world's fastest zip wire. As the sun sets, they meet a coast craft expert, who teaches them essential survival skills on one of the island's many picturesque beaches - and it's quickly evident they'd not last more than few hours if left to fend for themselves. Narrated by Olivia Colman

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: It's a big day for the vets and volunteers, as Harriet is called out to an emergency operation on an elephant whose leg has been wounded by a poacher's snare. Volunteer Debbie is put to the test as nine orphaned baby monkeys arrive at once, and Emma becomes surrogate mum to three-week-old baby hippo, Hector

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Farmer's daughter Mandy Sowray and her friend Anny Harrison host first at Laskill Country House in Helmsley, North Yorkshire. Chris Witton and Saroop Ram from the Swan Hotel feel right at home, but tech-savvy Colin and Ben Warburg from Scalford Hall Hotel fail to connect from the start. During the afternoon's hay-bale race, youngsters Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker from the Abbey Hotel fail to stack up to the challenge. At dinner, Mandy bigs up her banger qualifications, but at breakfast she fails to crack Chris's order. Colin's feedback leaves unanswered questions for Mandy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris Witton and his spiritual friend Rammy hope that straightforward value for money will win them the competition, as they host at the Swan Hotel in Telford. A Yorkshire pudding-making competition leaves confident cooks Mandy and Anny thinking there's more to youngsters Charlie and Lauren than meets the eye. At dinner, Rammy opens up about his troubled past. Colin has a rude awakening in the morning. At feedback time, Chris has a change of heart about some of his guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Colin Warburg and his student son Ben hope that their 81-room country pile - Scalford Hall Hotel in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire - will wow their rivals. However, Chris and Rammy are still smarting from their feedback and think that Colin is playing games. After an afternoon crate stacking competition on the hotel's high wire course, Colin gets a grilling at dinner. A lukewarm reaction to breakfast only adds to Colin's woes and when he sees his feedback he suspects that retribution is at hand

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Abbey Hotel in Battle, East Sussex, run by confident young colleagues Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker. However when Charlie and Lauren's guests arrive, Colin and Ben feel the squeeze and Chris and Rammy find themselves in a sticky situation. On an afternoon at the archery range, scores are settled. At dinner, straight-talking Chris can't help but be impressed, but when the conversation turns to parking, the guests are in a jam with their hosts. Later, Colin's feedback leaves Lauren wanting answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up to settle some scores. Laskill Country House host Mandy wants to crack where she went wrong with breakfast and why Colin and Ben failed to connect with her, while Chris and Rammy from The Swan Hotel wish to question Lauren on her room flaws and get to the bottom of Colin's bedroom nightmares

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: New chateau owners Jayne and Steve need to get four en suite themed bedrooms ready for their new B&B business. For the Oriental suite, Jayne goes to visit Angel for help and advice. Plus, a chance to meet Brit Justin and his Aussie wife Annabelle, who are turning their chateau, with its sweeping views of the Pyrenees, into a family home

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to help get the original chateau pizza oven working, while Annabelle and Justin return from holiday to find their chateau infested with bees

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel goes shopping with Stephanie and shows her the best way to frame her father's original artwork using modern and antique fabric. Justin and Annabelle put up their newly renovated exterior chateau shutters, but the numbering seems to have gone a bit haywire, so getting the shutters on the right windows proves a challenge

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Baksho wants to buy a holiday home in the resort of Gran Alacant on Spain's popular Costa Blanca. With a budget of £100,000 and her sister Sukvinder by her side to help, Jasmine Harman sets out to find Baksho the perfect family friendly property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Selina and her sister Reena look for a holiday home in Mijas Costa on the Costa del Sol within a budget of £100,000 that has a garden and a sea or mountain view

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Debbie and Dave from West Sussex fancy owning a holiday home in the Dordogne region of France, so Jonnie Irwin lines up five properties within their £200,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps a couple from Kent find a home on Spain's Orihuela Costa on a budget of £100,000, with a golf course within easy reach

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Carol longs to buy a holiday home for her and her husband on the island of Lanzarote that will work for all four generations of their family. She particularly loves the east coast, with its buzzing resorts of Costa Teguise and Puerto del Carmen, and is looking for a town house or apartment with a pool and a view from the terrace. With a budget of £150,000 - and Carol's daughter-in-law-Donna coming along to help - Jasmine Harman sets out to find a property to please everyone

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle (2017)
programme.desc: Four high school students are drawn into the jungle setting of a video game. However, their digital alter egos are not what they expected. Fantasy action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Self-made millionaire Delia is looking for a man to build a future with - could City trader Mark be the one? Bengy has a trauma from his past he is reluctant to share - luckily Lara is a caring soul with a listening ear. Butcher Nick is excited at the thought of meeting someone, even though he admits he doesn't know how to speak to women

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the rising incidence of theft in Northamptonshire, where in 2018 burglaries hit a five-year high. PC Imogen Robinson chases a prolific shoplifter who has stolen a box of chocolate bars from a shop. PC Ben Norris is dispatched to apprehend a suspected burglar who then carries out a rooftop protest

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shws including Line of Duty, Snackmasters, Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow, Mastermind, Baby Surgeons: Delivering Miracles, The Art of the Joy of Sex and Dr Pimple Popper. Plus, they observe BBC news coverage of the apparent spat between Dominic Cummings and Boris Johnson

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett in the words-and-numbers game, while Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson meets 21-year-old YouTuber Jay from London, who has no problem messaging women online but cannot make connections in real life, and 28-year-old Victoria from Liverpool, who is looking for her very own Xena, Warrior Princess. Will the back-to-basics approach of Naked Attraction help either of them find what they're looking for?

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix sets two fine dining chefs the challenge of creating a perfect replica of the Snickers bar. The combination of chocolate, peanuts, caramel and nougat may sound simple, but it proves to be a marathon challenge for Tom Brown, who has his own restaurant in London's trendy Hackney Wick, and Sabrina Gidda, who runs the kitchen at the all-female members' club AllBright in Mayfair. After weeks of trials and tribulations, the rivals cook their replicas from scratch for the factory bigwigs, who will choose the winner

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty hops over to France to find out what the white skin on Brie is made of. Helen Lawal explores how candy floss is inspiring a food revolution, while Briony May Williams goes crayfish trapping in a bid to learn why it is not possible to buy them whole in the supermarkets

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week comes from Cliff House Hotel in Southbourne, where owner Rich Norris and chef Matt Head hope their decor and top-notch breakfast will give them the edge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second venue of the week is the Hotel Barge Waternimf in Ely, Cambridgeshire, which has an open-plan kitchen leaving the hosts exposed to everything the guests have to say

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Montpellier Hotel in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where Hannah Henewaa and Ibrahim Sani hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Fiancees and live-in landlords Paul Wright and Dave McDermott welcome their rivals to the Coach and Horses in Compton in West Sussex, where they hope that their touch of country class will be the cream of the crop. When the guests arrive, Hannah and Ibrahim drink it all in, but the decor comes up short for Matt and Rich and a bad night's sleep doesn't leave Ibrahim in high spirits. At breakfast, the fry-up falls flat for some of the guests, and the feedback leaves a bitter taste in the hosts' mouths

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. First up are Rich and Matt from the Cliff House Hotel, and after tackling Paul and Dave's complaints about the lack of blinds in the bathroom, it's another pair's payment that could prove curtains for their chances in the competition

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to check out the new boiler for her mum's apartment - but it's bad news and mum Isobel is not happy. Justin and Annabelle must get busy to complete rooms in time for a visit from Justin's 95-year-old grandmother. Jayne and Steve battle with a giant four poster bed for the master en suite bedroom, but with a little help from some friends, manage to complete another room

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie finishes her mother Isobel's apartment. Painted and decorated, it now has her fathers framed pictures on the walls, a stunning 200-year-old mahogany canopy, plus furniture. Elsewhere, Jayne and Steve finish the Oriental Room and are ready to take in guests

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eatery's failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows five properties to Rachel and Miguel, who have sold their house in Dorset and are now planning a permanent move to Spain's highly sought-after Orihuela Costa

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which a property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad. Jasmine Harman is on Spain's Costa Blanca to help Wendy and Lee find a traditional countryside property. The couple, their two children, their pet Doberman and their West African parrot are planning a permanent move to Spain and would like a four-bedroom detached house with plenty of outside space, all for their budget of £150,000. Can Jasmine find their perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Lancashire couple Derek and Jane find a dream home in Chiclana de la Frontera, Spain, showing them five properties to accommodate all their grown-up children

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across America saving failing restaurants from disaster. In the first of two visits to Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, the chef encounters Joe Nagy, who claims he has the best menu from New York to Los Angeles. However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brothers Peter and David share a home in Wolverhampton and have decided to jointly buy a holiday home in the Spanish town of Torrevieja. With a budget of £100,000, they want a home that not only ticks all their boxes but also suits their large extended family too, so turn to Jean Johansson for advice

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Will Hardie and George struggle to bring ancient Roman technology back to life and the build on their log cabin in the wilderness begins in earnest, while the road trip around Italy takes in an impressive fire station. The architect also meets David Moreton, a former Paralympic swimmer hoping to turn an old air ambulance into an amphibious crash pad for his charity fundraising tours, and DIY enthusiasts Ben and Michelle, who want to build a summerhouse for free, using hundreds of disused shipping pallets

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: After 15 years of saving and planning, ecologist Fred and communications manager Saffron Baker start building work on their new family home in a village in the Peak District. However, the couple face difficulties as the start of construction proves more a feat of civil engineering than a house build, and work comes to a halt when their self-build mortgage falls through

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet a couple in Rushden in Northamptonshire who are unsure whether they should renovate their current home or sell up and move into another. Susan and John are torn over their unique five-bed character home, so Kirstie and Phil offer their expert advice in an effort to help them make the right decision for their family's future

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in Sheffield. Scientists Lois and Ahmed's strategic approach has so far seen them lose out on eight houses, being outbid by just a few thousand pounds each time. With a toddler in tow, and living with Lois's parents, the heat is on for Kirstie to find their own family home. Phil is with Liz and Luke, who are set on the rural areas around the city, close to the Peak District

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The team are facing one of the busiest days in Barnsley Casualty's history, and the pressure mounts on staff members as patients await beds to become available, so self-named Emergency Clown Sister Jane helps to give the younger nurses a moral boost during this difficult period. Meanwhile, consultant doctor Jane treats 10-year-old Oliver, who suffers from a rare condition called Angelman Syndrome

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: When the Beast from the East and storm Emma collide, Paddington's managers debate whether to close the station completely, something that has happened on very few occasions since World War II. There is excitement among the staff as the Duke and Duchess of Sussex board a train to Cardiff, but a rail defect at Didcot threatens to upset their journey

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The officers deal with a number of uninsured drivers, including one who leads them on a 35-minute chase and another who flees the scene of a rush-hour crash, earning himself a full-scale investigation for his trouble. Then in Bradford, a crackdown on road offences leads to the officers nabbing another insurance avoider, but tensions run high as a result

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: It is estimated that more than a million vehicles in Britain are fitted with cloned number plates as criminals who switch them to pass by police cameras undetected. On the outskirts of Bradford, a suspected stolen car using cloned plates crashes after running a red light, leaving the occupants of another car with serious injuries, leaving officers to deal with the aftermath, while a manhunt is launched for the offenders

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jason heads to a Christmas party that has turned into a full-blown riot, while Adam and Nick catch a drunken woman giving her daughter a lift to the shops. Plus, Kev is seconds away from disaster when he places himself in the path of danger to prevent a car from becoming a fireball

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A runaway suspect puts Gary's life in danger, Scott and Adam discover a huge stash of drugs in the boot of a car, and officers are called to a New Year's Eve pub brawl. Plus, Ady catches a car thief, and five police vehicles are involved in a motorway chase

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Dave use GPS technology to track a pair of mobile-phone thieves, and a car crashes into a house during the Christmas period. Plus, Nick pursues a criminal gang, Vicky deals with a van containing suspicious-looking scrap, and Jason contends with his own traffic nightmare

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Barnsley Hospital treats the largest number of patients in a single shift in its history, including a man being rushed in with a suspected brain injury. Health care assistant Sharon Bailey and volunteer Jane Allen try to keep their colleagues' spirits up with tea and cake, while a nine-year-old girl in the paediatric triage area does not want to stay overnight and a strange smell emerges from the lost property box

programme.desc: Dean brings Mackenzie to her scheduled abortion, worried about Ari who is out of the loop. Lewis considers filing a report against Christian

programme.desc: Series in which two families exchange holidays to see if money can buy happiness. programme.title: Predator: Catching The Black Cab Rapist
programme.desc: Documentary offering new analysis of the investigation into the prolific serial rapist John Worboys, who drugged, raped and sexually assaulted over 100 victims in the back of his black cab between 2000 and 2008. With access to some of his victims, and the police team that finally cracked the case once flaws in the original investigation emerged, this film's new analysis of the facts explains why it took years before Worboys's long and violent campaign of terror against women was finally brought to an end

programme.title: Millionaire Age Gap Love
programme.desc: Part one of two. The popularity of age-gap relationships among the super-rich, meeting six millionaire couples who are decades apart. Stories include that of 37-year-old Carolyn, who landed a job at 54-year-old multi-millionaire John's insurance company, little knowing that within a year she would be engaged to her boss and promoted to CEO of Mansfield Town football club. Meanwhile, DIY millionaire Craig had his life turned upside down when he won the UK's first Big Brother. But now, aged 48, nothing could have prepared him for his whirlwind romance with 33-year-old Laura

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall comes to the aid of three generations of the same family whose harmonious existence is being disrupted by a running feud between two of their dogs. Plus, a Border Collie's obsession with mirrors is becoming worrying, and a Chinese Crested will not let any man near his owner

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital, beginning as severe weather conditions place it under even more stress than usual. Dr Julian tends to pensioner Brian who was brought in after breaking his ankle walking his dog in the snow, and the medic also sees former miner Andrew, who has a chronic lung condition. Paediatric consultant Dr Jane is called into action when four-year-old Teddy is rushed in after gashing his head in a sledging accident

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Staffing problems hit the department as several nurses call in sick, and young manager Tom has to use his charm to get the team to commit to working extra shifts. Paramedics are concerned about a family that were involved in a road traffic accident, and consultant Julian treats a critically ill patient with undiagnosed symptoms

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The team are facing one of the busiest days in Barnsley Casualty's history, and the pressure mounts on staff members as patients await beds to become available, so self-named Emergency Clown Sister Jane helps to give the younger nurses a moral boost during this difficult period. Meanwhile, consultant doctor Jane treats 10-year-old Oliver, who suffers from a rare condition called Angelman Syndrome

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Barnsley Hospital treats the largest number of patients in a single shift in its history, including a man being rushed in with a suspected brain injury. Health care assistant Sharon Bailey and volunteer Jane Allen try to keep their colleagues' spirits up with tea and cake, while a nine-year-old girl in the paediatric triage area does not want to stay overnight and a strange smell emerges from the lost property box

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A critically ill patient is brought into the emergency department after suffering a sudden cardiac arrest while in a local cafe with his wife. Meanwhile, doctor Bharath Reddy treats Maxine, who broke her wrist when she slipped de-icing the car, and another patient is treated after fracturing her shoulder

programme.title: Sarah Payne: 5 Mistakes That Caught a Killer
programme.desc: Examining the murder of Sarah Payne, an eight-year-old girl who was killed by Roy Whiting in 2000, and the mistakes he made that led to him being arrested and locked up for life. The programme features interviews with detectives, forensic experts and other crucial witnesses, charting the police investigation and the evidence that led them to her killer

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: In January 2011, 48-year-old Carole Waugh decided to give online dating a try. Carole was a successful and independently wealthy woman, but was searching for more excitement in her life and a partner to share it with. After months of searching, Carole finally met someone she thought was her perfect match - rich doctor Rakesh Bhayani. But all was not as Carole believed. Bhayani was not a doctor, but a convicted conman who only had eyes for Carole's money. Less than a year later, Bhayani would take not only her money, but also her life

programme.title: 5 Clues That Caught the Killer: Rhys Jones
programme.desc: Documentary examining the investigation into the murder of 11-year-old Rhys Jones, a schoolboy shot dead on his way home from football training in 2007. The programme looks at how police managed to make the vital breakthroughs that finally put Rhys' killer behind bars, featuring interviews with police officers and specialists directly involved in the case

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: The case of a London gang who murdered 17-year-old Marcel Addai after an ongoing feud on social media, which saw gang members threatening each other in homemade rap videos

programme.title: Murdered By
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of 23-year-old Molly McLaren by her ex-boyfriend Joshua Stimpson, who stabbed her 75 times in broad daylight in June 2017

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at some of the most terrifying disasters in recent history, from horrifying dramas at sea to one of the worst air disasters in British aviation history, using eye witness testimony and footage taken by those involved telling the human stories behind the events. A cruise ship sailing off the coast of Norway is caught in a dangerous storm, and a passenger witnesses the casing of an airplane engine being ripped off mid-flight

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a gay man beaten to death during the abduction of his adopted infant son. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pregnant loan officer is found murdered in the boot of her car and the trail leads to drugs and a professional basketball player, whose ties to the victim appear to extend beyond the financial deal she approved for him. Detective drama, with Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a crooked car dealer whose son is dying of leukaemia presents McCoy and Ross with a moral dilemma. His ex-wife is their prime suspect - but it transpires that the victim had regularly abused her and refused to pay child support. Drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the death of a woman killed in what appears to be a road rage incident. However, as they retrace her last actions, they suspect she was accidentally murdered by an assassin following the wrong car. The intended victim turns out to be a reporter claiming to have uncovered a plot to rig an election by a senator, who Briscoe suspects of having connections with the Mafia

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The body of a female jogger is found in a park, and detectives Briscoe and Green discover she was mauled to death by a pit-bull terrier. The race is on to trace the animal and its owner before another tragedy occurs - and the team is shocked to discover a warehouse where dogs are trained for illegal fighting. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The officers leap into action when a financial planner is killed in her apartment building. It soon becomes apparent that the culprit could be a hit-man who was intending to murder someone else who happened to resemble the victim. Crime drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS headquarters is evacuated after the team discovers a body as well as bomb materials on the roof of the squad room. Meanwhile, the first draft of Ducky's book comes under scrutiny

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is granted protective custody of a 10-year-old orphaned refugee, Elena, when an NCIS case reveals she is the target of a violent gang. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team searches for a petty officer suspected of assault, who escapes when the van transporting him crashes into a lake. Torres works with a witness who heard vital evidence

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee reluctantly visits his high school after the unique computer password he used as a teenager is linked to the murder of a Department of Defense contractor. The case escalates when an intruder breaks into McGee's home in an attempt to steal his archaic high school computer

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Eames and Goren's investigation into the murder of a pharmaceutical salesman found cut in half uncovers a web of corruption involving fraud, bribery, identity theft and a conspiracy to sell contaminated blood. Detective drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A girl takes desperate measures to avenge the years of abuse and neglect inflicted upon her by her psychotherapist parents. programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent

Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

Crime drama, starring AJ Buckley, Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

However, one of the passengers is planning to kill his lover to stop her blackmailing him, and has arranged a cast-iron alibi. But when the crime is discovered, the captain looks to Columbo to solve the mystery. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Vaughn, Dean Stockwell, Patrick Macnee and Poupee Bocar

programme.title: Columbo: It's All in the Game (1993)
programme.desc: A wealthy socialite and her daughter discover they have been seeing the same man, and have him killed. When Columbo investigates the case, the mother is determined to make sure all his attention is on her and not her daughter, and an unlikely romance develops between sleuth and suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Faye Dunaway

programme.title: Columbo: Death Lends a Hand (1971)
programme.desc: The crumpled cop investigates when a private eye murders his client's adulterous wife because she refuses to submit to his blackmail threats, and arranges the crime scene to make it look as if she was the victim of a mugging gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ray Milland and Robert Culp

programme.title: Columbo: Etude in Black (1972)
programme.desc: An orchestra conductor murders his mistress for threatening to reveal their affair, and makes her death look like suicide. However, he reckons without the intervention of crumpled cop Columbo, who sees through the subterfuge and determines to get to the truth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, John Cassavetes, Myrna Loy and Blythe Danner

programme.title: Columbo: Fade Into Murder (1976)
programme.desc: An actor who plays a detective on a soap opera is being blackmailed by his ex-lover, who is also his producer. Convinced that playing a sleuth makes him clever enough to get away with murder, he kills her and frames her husband - but has reckoned without the intervention of the crumpled cop. Crime drama, with Peter Falk and William Shatner

programme.title: Columbo: A Matter of Honour (1976)
programme.desc: The sleuth's holiday in Mexico is disrupted by a bizarre death involving a suave retired bullfighter, who has killed his best friend using one of the animals as a murder weapon - but is determined his spotless image and reputation will remain untarnished. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ricardo Montalban, A Martinez and Pedro Armendariz Jr

programme.title: Columbo: Requiem for a Falling Star (1973)
programme.desc: A vicious gossip columnist digs the dirt on an ageing film actress, who responds by taking on her most challenging role yet - as a real-life murderer. But when the actress kills her own assistant in error, she looks for a way to shift the blame. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Anne Baxter, Mel Ferrer, Kevin McCarthy and multi-Oscar-winning costume designer Edith Head in a guest appearance as herself

programme.title: Columbo: Butterfly in Shades of Grey (1993)

Crime drama, with Peter Falk, William Shatner and Molly Hagan

programme.desc: The team heads to the Kelvingrove Museum and Art Gallery in Glasgow, where James Lewis is surprised by the contents of a small circular box and Will Axon focuses on four silk postcards sent from the battlefields during the First World War. Plus, Paul Martin visits artist and willow weaver Lise Bech and tries his hand at basket making

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lyndsey and Paul from Glasgow are newly engaged and want to kick start the next chapter of their lives together by buying a family holiday home in the area around Oliva in eastern Spain. Ben Hillman leaves no stone unturned to find property contenders for their budget of £50,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps good friends Mike Dawson and Louise Clark search for a holiday apartment in the resort of Sitges, near Barcelona, on a budget of £170,000. Louise is hoping for somewhere to escape with her two-year-old daughter, while Mike is looking to make the first step in a potentially permanent move

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Air Ambulance medics rush to the scene where a man has been crushed by his milk truck. The crew also help a biker who crashed at speed on the motorway

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: Pilot Scott conducts a town-centre landing after a pedestrian is hit by a car. A first aid course ends in a real-life emergency when a tennis player has a cardiac arrest on court

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: Thick fog complicates the Yorkshire Air Ambulance's attempts to help a man who has suffered nasty fall, while a motorcyclist is treated for leg injuries

programme.title: Helicopter ER

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The story of the volunteer lifeboat crews and lifeguards of the RNLI who rescue thousands of people and save hundreds of lives around our coastline and on the River Thames every year. In the first episode, a father and daughter find themselves treading water for almost an hour hundreds of metres out at sea, and three fishermen swim for their lives as their trawler is smashed onto rocks in Ireland. Elsewhere in London, an unlucky tourist is minutes from hypothermia after falling into the Thames

programme.desc: Everyone thinks a Bigfoot has stolen Lawrence's special shell when it disappears

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra and his friends take part in the Island Cup competition

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: UBOS
programme.desc: Cassy, Vern & Gus are gifted students who go to an enchanted school to learn about magic. After receiving a book named UBOS (Ultimate Book of Spells) they soon begin their journey of a lifetime

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Part one of two. A tornado sweeps up the Drakkar and takes it to China - where a general arrests the Vikings, demanding to know how they made their ship fly

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Part two of two. The vikings' attempt to make it back to the coast is thwarted - but Vic helps them escape by coming up with a way to teach the general to fly

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef explores quirky homespun food trends by visiting a a shop that creates custom preserves, and a home-based salmon smokehouse

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef enjoys a champagne afternoon tea, before she discovers the secret behind delicious fish and chips

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike is having financial trouble, so Laura decides to help out. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike's van and where he can park it interferes in the development of his relationship with Laura. Judi Dench and Michael Williams star

programme.title: Charlton Heston Presents the Bible
programme.desc: The renowned actor serves as storyteller an ancient Roman amphitheatre and other locales in Israel provide the settings, bringing the King James text to life

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams recounts the extraordinary tale of a Chicago-style turf-war gunfight on the Hampshire-Wiltshire border during the Second World War and Ennis Smith reveals how she broke the rules to marry her soldier boyfriend Clarence

programme.title: London's Screen Archives Presents
programme.desc: A special compilation of historical short films from Film London's London Screen Archives. London Ambulance Service Launch at County Hall. Life in Belmont, Sutton

programme.title: Miracle in Soho (1957)
programme.desc: A cynical road-repairer falls for the alluring charms of an Italian girl working as a barmaid, only to discover she and her family are planning to move to Canada. Romantic drama written by Emeric Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Belinda Lee and Cyril Cusack

programme.title: Heavens Above! (1963)
programme.desc: An idealistic vicar working as a prison chaplain is accidentally reassigned to a conservative middle-class village. He immediately puts his progressive ideas into practice, but finds his genuine belief in charity does not go down well with his snobbish congregation. Satirical comedy, starring Peter Sellers and Eric Sykes

programme.title: The Kid From Canada (1958)
programme.desc: A boy visits his family in the Scottish Highlands, where his boastfulness gets him into trouble during a horse riding trip. Family drama, with Christopher Braden and Bernard Braden

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Ray Townsend meets a bamboo expert during an enforced pit-stop in a Japanese lay-by. Back at Kew, Phil Griffiths has his work cut out creating a display featuring a tower of hundreds of pumpkins, and the Gardens' scholarship art student exhibits her intricate botanical designs. Alan Titchmarsh narrates

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Randall Keynes, the great-great-grandson of Charles Darwin, visits arboretum head Tony Kirkham in his search for a particular species of rhododendron to restock the naturalist's former home at Down House, and an archaeological dig poses a few delicate questions

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: The Escort (2015)
programme.desc: A sex-obsessed journalist whose career is falling apart has a chance encounter in a bar with a high-class prostitute. Seeing a chance to write the story that will make him a success, he persuades her to let him document her life, and a strange connection develops between the two lonely outsiders. Comedy drama, starring Michael Doneger, Lyndsy Fonseca and Bruce Campbell

programme.title: Felicity (1978)
programme.desc: A teenager in a remote Catholic girls' school seeks solace from her repressive life in reading erotic novels and having a secret love affair with one of her classmates. A trip to Hong Kong organised by her parents opens her eyes to more experiences - both good and bad. Australian drama, starring Glory Annen and Jody Hanson

programme.title: Sparks and Embers (2015)
programme.desc: A French woman living in London ends her relationship with her boyfriend when she decides to return to her own country. On the day she is due to leave, they meet again for one last goodbye. Despite knowing their break-up is for the best, both find themselves tempted to stay together - but neither feels brave enough to say it. Romantic comedy, starring Kris Marshall and Annelise Hesme

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: The family are horrified when Uncle Sonny makes an impromptu visit from Jamaica, and set about trying to come up with ways of getting him to leave - but when a pep talk fails and he brings a goat into their home, they are left with no option but to call on Grandma Sylvia for help. Mona Hammond and Rudolph Walker star

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael throws a fancy dress party at his house to celebrate the 33rd anniversary of the film Zulu, but the mood turns sour when the Monopoly board comes out

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The street rallies round to help as Mrs Hugett recuperates from a bout of alcohol poisoning, and Dirk is convinced Len the Gardener is trying to murder him

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Dirk is increasingly paranoid over Len's attempts to kill him, seeking solace in Jack's house, and Jimmy finds himself in huge amounts of debt to Mick and Keith as his drink problem grows

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Joe returns to the street after having a lobotomy, while Mrs Hugett comes up with a plan to get money out of Jack. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.desc: A newly qualified vet struggles to compete against an unscrupulous rival. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Peggy Cummins, Hattie Jacques and James Booth

programme.title: A Dozen Summers (2015)
programme.desc: Two 12-year-old twin sisters hijack a movie shoot and try to turn it into a film about their lives. Children's comedy, starring real-life sisters Scarlet and Hero Hall

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. Drama, based on the novel by Mary Hayley Bell, starring Alan Bates, Hayley Mills, Bernard Lee and Norman Bird

programme.title: The Lady with the Lamp (1951)
programme.desc: Biopic of Florence Nightingale, who went down in medical history for her heroic efforts to treat thousands of casualties during the Crimean War, while the British Government belittled her for being a woman. Starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events, showcasing London's vibrant culture scene

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

Drama, starring Paul McGann, Rebecca De Mornay and Derrick O'Connor

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)
programme.desc: A gang of lads selling drugs in Darndale in Dublin's Northside sets out to gain more money and power and enter the big leagues of the narcotics trade. Things begin to unravel quickly as not everyone in the area is willing to let them achieve the notoriety they crave without a fight. Thriller, starring John Connors and Fionn Walton

programme.title: Talking Points

programme.title: Inside Claridge's
programme.desc: Thirty delegations from around the world arrive to stay at the hotel for the duration of the Olympics, with the red carpet being rolled out five times a day for heads of state. World-famous Danish eatery Noma sets up a pop-up restaurant in the ballroom and Claridge's chefs are faced with providing Nordic-foraged food to thousands of guests who have paid £195 each. A couple fly in from Atlanta for the wedding of their dreams and a butler prepares a suite for one of the hotel's most valued guests, bringing furniture, hat boxes and 20 suits out of storage for just one night

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Forty Thousand Horsemen (1941)
programme.desc: Three Australian volunteers take part in the last cavalry charge in modern warfare while serving in Palestine. First World War drama, starring Grant Taylor and Betty Bryant

programme.title: A Dozen Summers (2015)
programme.desc: Two 12-year-old twin sisters hijack a movie shoot and try to turn it into a film about their lives. Children's comedy, starring real-life sisters Scarlet and Hero Hall

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court

programme.title: Heavens Above! (1963)
programme.desc: An idealistic vicar working as a prison chaplain is accidentally reassigned to a conservative middle-class village. He immediately puts his progressive ideas into practice, but finds his genuine belief in charity does not go down well with his snobbish congregation. Satirical comedy, starring Peter Sellers and Eric Sykes

programme.title: Hell Is a City (1960)
programme.desc: A tough criminal escapes from jail and hatches a scheme to rob a wealthy bookie, little realising a hardened copper is hot on his trail. Thriller, starring Stanley Baker, John Crawford, Donald Pleasence and Billie Whitelaw

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Fitzrovia for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events, showcasing London's vibrant culture scene

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: The Face of Love (2013)
programme.desc: A widow begins a new relationship with a man who is the exact double of her husband, and shares many of his personality traits. Over time, she grows to love his own unique qualities, but fears he will leave her if he ever discovers the resemblance. Romantic drama, starring Annette Bening, Ed Harris and Robin Williams

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Vengeance: A Love Story (2017)
programme.desc: A single mother is gang raped while her daughter is forced to watch, but the perpetrators escape justice by attacking her character in court. A police officer who was called to the crime scene assembles a group of strangers to help the woman get vengeance on her attackers. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage and Anna Hutchison

programme.title: The Seasoning House (2012)
programme.desc: A deaf-mute Balkans orphan is kidnapped and forced to work in a brothel, looking after the women who have been imprisoned there as sex slaves for the military. She puts her knowledge of the building's crawlspaces to use in planning revenge against those responsible for her suffering. Thriller, starring Rosie Day and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Camden and Sydenham for locals and tourists.

programme.desc: Thirty delegations from around the world arrive to stay at the hotel for the duration of the Olympics, with the red carpet being rolled out five times a day for heads of state. World-famous Danish eatery Noma sets up a pop-up restaurant in the ballroom and Claridge's chefs are faced with providing Nordic-foraged food to thousands of guests who have paid £195 each. A couple fly in from Atlanta for the wedding of their dreams and a butler prepares a suite for one of the hotel's most valued guests, bringing furniture, hat boxes and 20 suits out of storage for just one night

programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided. Brentford agonisingly missed out on automatic promotion on the final day of the regular campaign, but recovered to defeat Swindon Town on penalties in their semi-final, while Yeovil were 2-1 aggregate victors over Sheffield United

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football
